EDCS-03000459.text_edition: \n Lucio Licinio Luci filio Galeria Montano Sarapioni origine Malacitano adlecto Cordubensi flamini divorum Augustorum provinciae Baeticae splendidissimus ordo Malacitanorum \n
EDCS-03000459.translation: To Lucius Licinius Montanus Sarapio, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, from Malaca, admitted as a citizen of Corduba, for life, the most distinguished order of the people of Malaca dedicated this to him as Flamen of the Divine Augusts of the province of Baetica.
EDCS-03000459.summary: This is a dedication by the city of Malaca to Lucius Licinius Montanus Sarapio, who served as Flamen of the Divine Augusts of Baetica.

EDCS-03000460.text_edition: \n Servilia Atsinna Mellariensis annorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03000460.translation: Servilia Atsinna of Mellaria, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03000460.summary: This is a tombstone for Servilia Atsinna, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-03300436.text_edition: \n Tiberius Publicius Martialis annorum LXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03300436.translation: Tiberius Publicius Martialis, aged 65, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03300436.summary: This is a tombstone for Tiberius Publicius Martialis, who died at the age of 65.

EDCS-03300441.text_edition: \n Albanius Herculeanus nnorum XXXVII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03300441.translation: Albanius Herculeanus, aged 37, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03300441.summary: This is a tombstone for Albanius Herculeanus, who died at the age of 37.

EDCS-03300447.summary: This inscription likely refers to Secundilla Mnester and a repeated honor or status, possibly related to her burial or a memorial.

EDCS-03300448.summary: This inscription records the lifespan of Flavius Firmanus, who lived for 47 years.

EDCS-03300451.text_edition: \n Omphe nnorum XXVII cara suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03300451.translation: Omphe, aged 27, beloved by her family, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03300451.summary: This is a tombstone for Omphe, who died at the age of 27 and was loved by her family.

EDCS-03300474.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Acilia Bonato, who died at 19.

EDCS-03300480.text_edition: \n Threpte cara suis mensium XI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03300480.translation: Threpte, beloved by her family, aged 11 months, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-03300480.summary: This is a tombstone for Threpte, who died at the age of 11 months and was loved by her family.

EDCS-03300494.text_edition: \n Caio Aemilio Nigro Annio Senecae filio Arvaco Galeria Sexitano flamini divorum Augustorum provinciae Baeticae amico rarissimo Aemili Ligurius et Italicus et Delius eximia pro liberalitate posuerunt\n
EDCS-03300494.translation: Aemili Ligurius, Italicus, and Delius erected this with exceptional generosity to Gaius Aemilius Niger Annianus Senecae filius, of the Arvaco tribe, of the Galeria tribe of Sextant, friend of the Flamen of the Divine Augusts of the province of Baetica, a most rare friend.
EDCS-03300494.summary: This is a dedication by Aemili Ligurius, Italicus, and Delius to their rare friend, Gaius Aemilius Niger Annianus Senecae filius, who was a flamen and citizen.

EDCS-03700364.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aquiliae Severae Gai filiae Papiriae Emeritensi annorum XLIX fecit Marcus Septumius sic Severus matri piissumae! hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-03700364.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Septumius Severus made this for his most devoted mother, Aquilia Severa, daughter of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, from Emerita, aged 49. Here she lies. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-03700364.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aquilia Severa, dedicated by her son Marcus Septumius Severus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-03700441.text_edition: \n Marco Rutilio Cosinio Marci filio Calvo procuratori Augusti patrimonii Baeticae \n
EDCS-03700441.translation: To Marcus Rutilius Cosinius Calvus, son of Marcus, procurator of the Emperor in charge of the imperial patrimony in Baetica.
EDCS-03700441.summary: An inscription honoring Marcus Rutilius Cosinius Calvus, who served as an imperial procurator in the province of Baetica, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-03700442.text_edition: \n Dis inferis vos rogo utei sic recipiates sic nomen Luxsia Auli Antesti filia caput cor cosiliom! valetudinem vitam membra omnia accedat morbo cotidea sic et sei sic faciatis votum quod faccio sic solvam vostris sic meretis sic\n
EDCS-03700442.translation: Spirits of the underworld, I beg you to receive my name, Luxsia, daughter of Aulus Antestius, my head, heart, judgment! my health, life, all my limbs; may she be afflicted by daily illness, and if you do this, I shall pay my vow with your due merits.
EDCS-03700442.summary: A magical invocation to the underworld deities by Luxsia, daughter of Aulus Antestius, likely a vow for health and a plea against illness, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-04900507.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Camullia Kaesonis Marci liberta Veneria annorum XXIII sit tibi terra levis Camullia Primula mater de suo fecit\n
EDCS-04900507.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Camullia Veneria, freedwoman of Camullia Kaeso, aged 23. May the earth lie lightly on you. Camullia Primula, her mother, made this at her own expense.
EDCS-04900507.summary: A funerary epitaph for Camullia Veneria, a freedwoman, dedicated by her mother Camullia Primula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000439.text_edition: \n Faustilla Flaviorum hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05000439.translation: Faustilla Flaviorum lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05000439.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Faustilla Flaviorum, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05500982.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Trebonius Modestus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, from Nertobriga, aged 56. Trebonia Caesia [dedicated this] to her patron.
EDCS-05500982.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Trebonius Modestus, dedicated by his patroness Trebonia Caesia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501042.translation: To Fabia Caesarea, aged 25. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Fabia Caesarea, her dearest and most devoted mother, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-05501042.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Caesarea, arranged by her mother, Fabia Caesarea, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501043.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Clarinio Trophimo nnorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05501043.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. To Clarinus Trophimus, aged 50. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-05501043.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clarinus Trophimus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501055.translation: To the Divine Caesar Augustus Vespasianus, censor. The municipium of Muniguense, by decree of the decurions, Lucius Aelius Fronto dedicated.
EDCS-05501055.summary: A dedication to the deified Emperor Vespasian by the municipality of Muniguense, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-05501056.translation: To the Divine Titus, son of the Divine Caesar Augustus, censor. The municipium of Muniguense, by decree of the decurions, [Lucius] Licinius Victor dedicated.
EDCS-05501056.summary: A dedication to the deified Emperor Titus by the municipality of Muniguense, likely dating to the late 1st century CE.

EDCS-05501081.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Apollonius, aged 37, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501081.summary: A tombstone for Apollonius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501089.text_edition: \n Lucio Cominio Vipsanio Salutari domo Roma perfectissimo viro a cognitionibus domini nostri Imperatoris Luci Septimi Severi Pertinacis Augusti procuratori provinciae Baeticae procuratori calendarii Vegetiani procuratori provinciae Siciliae procuratori alimentorum per Apuliam Calabriam Lucaniam Bruttios subprocuratori ludi magni optimo viro et integrissimo Irenaeus Augusti nostri verna dispensator portus Ilipensis praeposito sanctissimo\n
EDCS-05501089.translation: To Lucius Cominius Vipsanius Salutaris, from Rome, a most accomplished man, in charge of petitions for our lord Emperor Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, procurator of the province of Baetica, procurator of the Vegetian calendar, procurator of the province of Sicily, procurator of the alimentary funds for Apulia, Calabria, Lucania, and the Bruttii, sub-procurator of the great games, a most excellent and upright man. Irenaeus, a freedman of our Emperor, steward of the port of Ilipa, most holy supervisor, [erected this].
EDCS-05501089.summary: A dedication to Lucius Cominius Vipsanius Salutaris by his freedman Irenaeus, listing numerous procuratorial and administrative posts held by Salutaris, likely dating to the reign of Septimius Severus (late 2nd to early 3rd century AD).

EDCS-05501090.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-05501090.translation: in Britain, prefect of the Cohors II Vasconum equitata, military tribune of Legio II Augusta, prefect of the Ala I Asturum, honored with the corona muralis and four golden crowns, also a vexillum and five hasta pura, honored by the armies in which he served with gilded chariots and equestrian statues, Quintus Fulvius Euchir, IIvir Augustalis, to his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-05501090.summary: An epitaph for Quintus Fulvius Euchir, a Roman military officer, detailing his career and awards, and mentioning his service in Britain, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501119.translation: To Magnus and Invictus, Emperor Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, holder of tribunician power, consul, father of the country, proconsul. The public administration of Italicum, devoted to his divine majesty, dedicates this, with Aurelius Julius, a most accomplished man, acting in place of the governor, supervised by Aurelius Ursus, an outstanding man, curator of the public administration of Italicum.
EDCS-05501119.summary: A dedication from the city of Italicum to the Emperor Marcus Annius Florianus, likely a usurper during the Crisis of the Third Century, possibly around 273-274 AD.

EDCS-05501124.text_edition: \n Caio Vallio Maximiano procuratori provinciarum Macedoniae Lusitaniae Mauretaniae Tingitanae fortissimo duci res publica Italicensium ob merita et quot sic provinciam Baeticam caesis hostibus paci pristinae restituerit Dedicata anno Licini Victoris et Fabi Aeliani IIvirororum pridie Kalendas Ianuarias\n
EDCS-05501124.translation: To Gaius Vallius Maximianus, procurator of the provinces of Macedonia, Lusitania, Mauretania Tingitana, a most valiant leader. The public administration of Italicum, for his merits and because he restored the province of Baetica to its former peace after defeating the enemies, dedicates this. Dedicated in the year of the IIviri Licinius Victor and Fabius Aelianus, on the day before the Kalends of January.
EDCS-05501124.summary: A dedication from the city of Italicum to Gaius Vallius Maximianus, praising his military achievements in Baetica and his administrative roles, dated to the joint consulship of Licinius Victor and Fabius Aelianus, likely in the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-05501183.translation: To Titus Flavius Polychrysus, freedman of the Emperor, most outstanding procurator of the Marian mountain, smelters of bronze.
EDCS-05501183.summary: A dedication to Titus Flavius Polychrysus, an imperial freedman and procurator, likely related to mining operations, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501184.translation: of the citizenship of the Romulenses of Malva.
EDCS-05501184.summary: A fragment indicating the origin or affiliation of an individual with the civitas Romulensium Malvensium, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501207.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aelius Aemilianus Rufus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501207.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Aelius Aemilianus Rufus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501265.text_edition: \n Marcus Accenna Marci filius Galeria Helvius Agrippa praetorius tribunus plebis legatus provinciae Africae dioecesis Carthaginensium item quaestori provinciae Africae IIIviro capitali tribuno laticlavio Syriae legionis XVI Flaviae item tribuno laticlavio Brittanniae legionis XX Valeriae victricis curio minor vixit annis XXXIIII mensibus tribus diebus XXIII Marcus Accenna Helvius Agrippa filius patri dulcissimo fecit\n
EDCS-05501265.translation: Marcus Accenna Helvius Agrippa, of the Galerian tribe, former praetorian, tribune of the plebs, legate of the province of Africa, of the diocese of Carthage, also quaestor of the province of Africa, capitalis IIIvir, laticlavian tribune of Syria with Legio XVI Flavia, also laticlavian tribune of Britain with Legio XX Valeria Victrix, junior curio, lived 32 years, 3 months, and 23 days. Marcus Accenna Helvius Agrippa, his son, made this for his dearest father.
EDCS-05501265.summary: An epitaph for Marcus Accenna Marci filius, detailing his extensive political and military career, and erected by his son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05501289.text_edition: \n Marco Cutio Marci filio Galeria Prisco Messio Rustico Aemilio Papo Arrio Proculo Iulio Celso consuli sodali Augustali legato pro praetore Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aelii Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii provinciae Dalmatiae curatori operum publicorum praefecto aerarii Saturni legato legionis XX Valeriae victricis curatori viae Aureliae praetori peregrino tribuno plebis quaestori pro praetore provinciae Africae tribuno militum legionis VII sic Augustae IIIIviro viarum curandarum Caesia Seni<l=I>la amico optimo \n
EDCS-05501289.translation: To Marcus Cutius Priscus Messius Rusticus Aemilius Papus Arrius Proculus Iulius Celsus, son of Marcus, of the Galerian tribe, consul, associate of Augustus, proconsular legate of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius for the province of Dalmatia, curator of public works, prefect of the Saturnian treasury, legate of Legio XX Valeria Victrix, curator of the Via Aurelia, praetor peregrinus, tribune of the plebs, quaestor pro praetore of the province of Africa, military tribune of Legio VII Claudia Augusta, IIvir for the maintenance of roads. Caesia Senila, to her best friend.
EDCS-05501289.summary: An epitaph for Marcus Cutius Priscus Messius Rusticus Aemilius Papus Arrius Proculus Iulius Celsus, detailing his distinguished career encompassing various high-ranking military, administrative, and priestly offices, erected by Caesia Senila, likely dating to the Antonine period (mid-2nd century AD).

EDCS-05501303.translation: Gaius Julius Sophro, aged 86 years, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501303.summary: A tombstone for Gaius Julius Sophro, who died at the age of 86.

EDCS-05501310.translation: Dedicated to the August Hercules. Quintus Castricius Re[---]parus made this offering.
EDCS-05501310.summary: A dedication to the god Hercules by Quintus Castricius Re[---]parus.

EDCS-05501311.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-05501311.translation: To Lucius Fabius Cordus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, a duovir, the people of the municipium of Cere [presented] 4 pairs of gladiators for the health and victory of the Caesars; the place and inscription were granted by decree of the decurions, confirmed by a document.
EDCS-05501311.summary: A decree from the town of Cere (or a similar place name) honoring Lucius Fabius Cordus by decree of the decurions for providing gladiatorial games.

EDCS-05501328.translation: Quintus Cornelius Maternus, aged 30 years, is buried here.
EDCS-05501328.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Cornelius Maternus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-05501345.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 7th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of the country, proconsul, by the victorious public association of Saeponenses.
EDCS-05501345.summary: An honorific inscription from the public association of Saeponenses dedicating a monument to Emperor Hadrian, likely from his 7th tribunician power period.

EDCS-05501346.translation: The victorious public association of Saeponenses, by decree of the decurions, made this offering to the deified Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, conqueror of Germans and Sarmatians, with Fabio Senecione and Fabio Pollione serving as duovirs.
EDCS-05501346.summary: A dedication by the public association of Saeponenses to the deified Emperor Marcus Aurelius, authorized by the decurions and erected during the duovirate of Fabio Senecione and Fabio Pollione.

EDCS-05501374.translation: Sacred to the Shades below. Hilara, daughter of Hilarius, pious, aged 60 years.
EDCS-05501374.summary: A funerary dedication to Hilara, daughter of Hilarius, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-05501376.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Augusti consulis VI filio divi Nervae consulis III tribunicia potestate II nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XII patri patriae consuli III Marcus Messius Rusticus Aemilius Papus Arrius Proculus Iulius Celsus sodalis Augustalis IIIIvir viarum curandarum tribunus militum legionis III Augustae quaestor pro praetore provinciae Africae tribunus plebis praetor peregrinus curator viae Aureliae legatus Augusti legionis XX Valeriae victricis optimo principi\n
EDCS-05501376.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan Augustus, consul VI, son of the deified Nerva, consul III, with tribunician power II, grandson, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power XII, father of the country, consul III, Marcus Messius Rusticus Aemilius Papus Arrius Proculus Iulius Celsus, Augustalis sodalis, duovir for roads, military tribune of the III Augustan legion, quaestor with propraetorian authority in the province of Africa, tribune of the plebs, praetor peregrinus, curator of the Aurelian Way, legate of Augustus of the XX Valeria Victrix legion, [dedicated this] to the best princeps.
EDCS-05501376.summary: An honorific inscription from Marcus Messius Rusticus and his colleagues to Emperor Trajan Hadrian, listing his titles and their own distinguished careers.

EDCS-05501377.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, Gaius Messius Quintus Trajan Decius Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, father of the country, with tribunician power II, consul II, proconsul, the city of Calles, devoted to his divine majesty, by decree of the decurions, [dedicated this].
EDCS-05501377.summary: An inscription from the city of Calles, by decree of its decurions, dedicating a monument to Emperor Trajan Decius.

EDCS-05501415.translation: [To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Valerius Aurelius Diocletianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power XX, consul VIII, father of the country, proconsul, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power XVIII, consul VI, father of the country, proconsul, and to Flavius Valerius Constantinus and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesars, [they] established [this].
EDCS-05501415.summary: A dedication honoring Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, along with the Caesars Constantius and Galerius, likely erected by an unnamed group, possibly officials, during Diocletian's 20th tribunician power period.

EDCS-05501436.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gelasinus vilicus XX libertatis vixit annos XLV Calpia Nimphe coniunx posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-05501436.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gelasinus, steward, lived 20 years after freedom; Calpia Nimphe erected this for her deserving husband.
EDCS-05501436.summary: A tombstone erected by Calpia Nimphe for her husband Gelasinus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501636.translation: Lucius Valerius Rufus, heir of Lucius Servilius Probus, gave this gift from his own money to his wife Canuleia, daughter of Quintus. Lucius Servilius Probus.
EDCS-05501636.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Valerius Rufus for his wife, Canuleia, likely during the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501645.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, twice conqueror of Armenia, with tribunician power for the 4th time, imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, proconsul, son of the deified Antoninus, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the deified Nerva, the republic of Ilurcis gave this gift by decree of its council, under the care of Vibianus.
EDCS-05501645.summary: A dedication by the city of Ilurcis to Emperor Lucius Aurelius Verus, likely dating to his reign (161-169 AD).

EDCS-05501676.text_edition: \n Marciae Cai filiae Celsae sacerdoti perpetuae domus divinae EX\n
EDCS-05501676.translation: To Marcia Celsa, daughter of Caius, perpetual priestess of the divine house.
EDCS-05501676.summary: An honorary inscription for Marcia Celsa, a perpetual priestess, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501678.translation: To our Lares. Suavis, freedman, and Faustus, steward, give this shrine and the Lares and Genius of the family, as their first gift from their own means.
EDCS-05501678.summary: A dedication to household gods (Lares and Genius) by a freedman and a steward, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501680.translation: Annia Salomonula, 1 year, 4 months, 1 day old. Jewess.
EDCS-05501680.summary: A tombstone for a young girl named Annia Salomonula, likely from the Imperial period, identifying her as Jewish.

EDCS-05501682.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Annius F[---].
EDCS-05501682.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription for Gaius Annius, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501691.translation: Politice, aged 50 years. Crysida, aged 5 years. Pusinnica, aged 5 years. They lie here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501691.summary: A tombstone for three individuals, Politice, Crysida, and Pusinnica, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501692.translation: Publicia Teriola lies here, aged 53 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501692.summary: A tombstone for Publicia Teriola, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501693.translation: Quieta, slave of Caius Mummius Marullus, aged 30 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-05501693.summary: A tombstone for Quieta, a slave of Caius Mummius Marullus, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05501694.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Victor, son of Fidus; his wife Acte, aged 25; his son Pyrallis; his daughter Clymene. Here lie. His son Byblis?
EDCS-05501694.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the burial of Victor, his wife, and children, with an uncertain addition for Byblis.

EDCS-05501704.translation: Verna, freedman of Lucius Valerius Laetus and Marcus Valerius Vetus, and Prima, freedwoman of Marcus Valerius Vetus, wife of Verna, willingly and deservedly fulfilled their vow to Salus, dedicated on the Kalends of March, when Gnaeus Cornelius Gaetulicus and Gaius Calvisius Sabinus were consuls.
EDCS-05501704.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made to the goddess Salus by freedmen and their families, dated to 85 AD by the consuls listed.

EDCS-05501711.text_edition: \n Venerem Augustam cum parergo item phialam argenteam Aemilii Rustici item trullam argenteam Marcus Annius Celtitanus testamento suo post mortem Aemiliae Arthemisiae uxoris et haeredis suae poni iussit Aemilia Arhtemisia filia posuit eademque suo annullum sic aureum gemma meliore\n
EDCS-05501711.translation: Marcus Annius Celtitanus, by his will after the death of his wife and heir Aemilia Arthemisia, ordered that a statue of Venus Augusta with attendant figures, a silver bowl belonging to Aemilius Rusticus, and a silver basin be placed here; Aemilia Arthemisia, his daughter, erected it and also placed here her own gold ring with a superior gem.
EDCS-05501711.summary: This funerary inscription records the posthumous erection of statues and silver objects by Marcus Annius Celtitanus, and the dedication of a ring by his daughter Aemilia Arthemisia.

EDCS-05600057.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, in his 30th year of tribunician power, chief priest, consul 13 times, father of the fatherland, made this.
EDCS-05600057.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Emperor Augustus, likely for a public work, dating to 1 BCE based on his tribunician power.

EDCS-05600060.translation: Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus Britannicus maximus Germanicus maximus, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, chief priest, in his 17th year of tribunician power, consul 4 times, proconsul, restored this.
EDCS-05600060.summary: This inscription by Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) records his restoration of a monument and lists his extensive imperial titles and lineage, dating to 214 AD.

EDCS-05600062.translation: Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, in his 3rd year of tribunician power, consul II, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest,...
EDCS-05600062.summary: This inscription records the joint authority of Emperors Valerian and Gallienus, likely for a public work, dating to around 255-257 AD.

EDCS-05600065.translation: Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, chief priest, in his 6th year of tribunician power...
EDCS-05600065.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Emperor Hadrian, mentioning his lineage and tribunician power, dating to 123 AD.

EDCS-05600066.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus Germanicus maximus Sarmaticus maximus...
EDCS-05600066.summary: This fragmentary inscription names Emperor Maximinus Thrax and lists some of his titles, dating to 236-238 AD.

EDCS-05600067.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, Sarmaticus maximus, Dacicus maximus...
EDCS-05600067.summary: This fragmentary inscription names Emperor Maximinus Thrax and lists some of his titles, dating to 237-238 AD.

EDCS-05600070.translation: To our lords Constantine and Constantius, most noble and blessed Caesars, born for the good of the state.
EDCS-05600070.summary: This inscription dedicates a monument to the emperors Constantine and Constantius, likely erected during their co-rule in the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-05600071.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine, consul for the 13th time, holding tribunician power for the 21st time, pontifex maximus, from the Baetis and Janus Augustus to the Ocean, 64 miles.
EDCS-05600071.summary: This milestone inscription, likely dating to 3 BCE, records a road measurement under Augustus.

EDCS-05600073.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine, consul for the 13th time, holding tribunician power for the 21st time, pontifex maximus, from the Baetis and Janus Augustus to the Ocean, 71 miles. To Constancy and Eternity of Augustus.
EDCS-05600073.summary: This milestone inscription, likely dating to 3 BCE, records a road measurement under Augustus and includes a dedication to Constancy and Eternity.

EDCS-05600075.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the divine, consul for the 13th time, holding tribunician power for the 21st time, pontifex maximus, from the Baetis and Janus Augustus to the Ocean, 77 miles.
EDCS-05600075.summary: This milestone inscription, likely dating to 3 BCE, records a road measurement under Augustus.

EDCS-05600082.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the divine Augustus, grandson of the divine Julius, pontifex maximus, consul V, emperor, holding tribunician power for the 37th time, from Janus Augustus who is at the Baetis to the Ocean, 64 miles.
EDCS-05600082.summary: This milestone inscription, likely dating to 37 CE, records a road measurement under Tiberius.

EDCS-05600083.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the divine Augustus, grandson of the divine Julius, pontifex maximus, consul V, emperor, holding tribunician power for the 37th time, from Janus Augustus to the Baetis, to the Ocean, 78 miles.
EDCS-05600083.summary: This milestone inscription, likely dating to 37 CE, records a road measurement under Tiberius.

EDCS-05600085.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the divine Augustus, grandson of the divine Julius, pontifex maximus, consul V, emperor, holding tribunician power for the 37th time, from Janus Augustus who is at the Baetis, to the Ocean, 82 miles.
EDCS-05600085.summary: This milestone inscription, likely dating to 37 CE, records a road measurement under Tiberius.

EDCS-05600086.translation: Gaius Caesar Germanicus, son of Germanicus Caesar, grandson of Tiberius Augustus, great-grandson of the divine Augustus, great-great-grandson of the divine Julius, Augustus, father of the fatherland, consul II, emperor, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, pontifex maximus, from the Baetis and Janus Augustus to the Ocean, 56 miles.
EDCS-05600086.summary: This milestone inscription, likely dating to 39 CE, records a road measurement under Caligula.

EDCS-05600088.text_edition: \n Sacrum Polluci Sextus Quintius Sexti Quinti Successini libertus Fortunatus ob honorem VIviratus ex decreto ordinis soluta pecunia petente populo donum de sua pecunia dato epulo civibus et incolis et circensibus factis dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-05600088.translation: Sacred to Pollux. Sextus Quintius Sexti Quintii Successini, freedman, Fortunatus, out of his own money, gave and dedicated this gift in honor of his tenure as a vigintivir, because the order decreed it and the people requested it, after providing a feast for the citizens and residents and staging games.
EDCS-05600088.summary: This dedication inscription, likely from the 1st century CE, records a freedman's gift to the god Pollux in honor of his public office and to entertain the populace.

EDCS-05600089.translation: Nero Claudius Augustus, son of the divine Claudius, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of the divine Augustus, great-great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar, holding tribunician power, emperor, consul [
EDCS-05600089.summary: This fragmentary inscription, likely from the reign of Nero (54-68 CE), begins to list his titles.

EDCS-05600090.translation: Emperor Caesar Domitianus Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Vespasian, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, emperor XXI, consul XV, censor, perpetual father of the fatherland, from the arch where the Via Augusta begins, restored [
EDCS-05600090.summary: This inscription, likely from 81-96 CE, records Domitian's restoration of the Via Augusta, listing his imperial titles and offices.

EDCS-05600091.translation: Emperor Caesar Domitianus Augustus, son of the divine Vespasian Augustus, Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 9th time, acclaimed imperator for the 21st time, consul for the 15th time, censor for life, father of the country.
EDCS-05600091.summary: This inscription records the titles and honors of Emperor Domitian, likely associated with a public monument from his reign.

EDCS-05600093.translation: Emperor Nerva Caesar Augustus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 3rd time, proconsul, father of the country. (He) restored (this).
EDCS-05600093.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Nerva, indicating his authority and likely marking the restoration of a public work.

EDCS-05600094.translation: Emperor Nerva Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the country, repaired and restored roads corrupted by age.
EDCS-05600094.summary: This inscription details the titles of Emperor Trajan and records his initiative to repair and restore damaged roads.

EDCS-05600095.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix Augustus Parthicus maximus, Britannicus maximus, Germanicus maximus, son of the divine Severus Pius, grandson of the divine Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the divine Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus and the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power.
EDCS-05600095.summary: This inscription enumerates the extensive titulature and honorific titles of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Felix.

EDCS-05600098.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, Germanicus maximus, Dacicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, holding tribunician power for the 3rd time, acclaimed imperator for the 2nd time, consul for the 1st time, father of the country, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar, Germanicus maximus, Dacicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, son of Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix, restored (this).
EDCS-05600098.summary: This inscription records the honors and titles of Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son, Caesar Maximus, likely commemorating a restoration project.

EDCS-05600099.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius Domitius Aurelianus, Pius Felix Invictus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power.
EDCS-05600099.summary: This inscription lists the titles of Emperor Aurelian, likely part of a monument from his reign.

EDCS-05600101.translation: To Nero Claudius, son of the divine Claudius, grandson of Germanicus Caesar, great-grandson of Tiberius Caesar Augustus, great-great-grandson of the divine Augustus, Caesar Augustus Germanicus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power for the 3rd time, imperator for the 3rd time, consul designated for the 3rd time. 222.
EDCS-05600101.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Nero, listing his imperial titles and lineage, likely from a monument commemorating his rule.

EDCS-05600298.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Augustus Divi filius consul XI tribunicia potestate XVI imperator XIIII pontufex maxumus\n
EDCS-05600298.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of a god, consul for the 11th time, holding tribunician power for the 16th time, imperator for the 14th time, pontifex maximus.
EDCS-05600298.summary: This inscription lists the imperial titles of an emperor, likely marking a significant public work or achievement.

EDCS-05600396.translation: To the genius of Lacimurga. Norbana Quintilla, daughter of Quintus, from Norba.
EDCS-05600396.summary: This dedication is to a local deity or spirit named Lacimurga, made by Norbana Quintilla.

EDCS-05600640.translation: In honor of the divine house. Gaius Aufustius Vegetus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, twice duovir, curator of the baths, and Gaius Aufustius Avitus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, designated duovir, son, (they) gave (this) from their own funds and dedicated (it) with the circus games.
EDCS-05600640.summary: This inscription records the construction and dedication of baths by two individuals, Gaius Aufustius Vegetus and Gaius Aufustius Avitus, likely during their tenure in local magistracies.

EDCS-05600641.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Pomponius Florus, aged 39, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His heirs made this.
EDCS-05600641.summary: A tombstone for Publius Pomponius Florus, a man who died at 39, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-05600642.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caelius Vernacellio, aged 51. Frontonia Vegeta made this for her most devoted husband. May he lie here, and may the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-05600642.summary: A tombstone for Caelius Vernacellio, a man who died at 51, erected by his devoted wife Frontonia Vegeta.

EDCS-05600673.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Marcia Venusta.

EDCS-05600685.translation: By decree of the decurions, Gaius(?)......Asidonensis(?)......
EDCS-05600685.summary: A fragment likely recording an honorific inscription by local council members, with an incomplete name and possible place of origin.

EDCS-05600693.translation: To Fabia Maura, daughter of Quintus, aged 32.
EDCS-05600693.summary: A funerary dedication for Fabia Maura, who died at age 32.

EDCS-05600732.translation: Publius Aemilius Daphnus, a sevir, gave the baths to the municipals of Murga at his own expense, and on the day he dedicated them, he gave a feast of one denarius to the citizens and residents. And he promised that as long as he lived, he would give one denarius on the same day to the same people. And for the upkeep of these baths, as long as he lived, he pledged 150 denarii annually.
EDCS-05600732.summary: An inscription recording the dedication of public baths and a related feast by Publius Aemilius Daphnus, a sevir, to the municipality of Murga, with a pledge for their ongoing maintenance.

EDCS-05600733.translation: Lucius Pedanius Venustus and Lucius Pedanius Clarus and Lucius Pedanius Lupus erected this for Porcia Maura, their most devoted wife and mother. They dedicated it with games in the circus, having received the plot from the state (?).
EDCS-05600733.summary: A funerary monument for Porcia Maura, erected by her husband and sons, who also celebrated her dedication with games and likely received the burial plot from public funds.

EDCS-05601279.translation: Gaius Caesar Germanicus, son of Germanicus Caesar, grandson of Tiberius Augustus, great-grandson of divine Augustus, great-great-grandson of divine Julius, Augustus, father of the country, consul twice, emperor, with tribunician power twice, chief priest, from Baetis and Janus Augustus to the Ocean, 62.
EDCS-05601279.summary: An honorific inscription detailing the extensive lineage and titles of Emperor Gaius Caesar Germanicus (Caligula), possibly marking a territorial extent or a building project.

EDCS-05601339.translation: Publius Vibius Probus, son of Lucius Broccus, aged 17, lies here.
EDCS-05601339.summary: A tombstone for Publius Vibius Probus, who died at age 17.

EDCS-05601343.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Fructuosus lived 18 years, 10 months. Pious among his own. May he lie here, may the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-05601343.summary: A tombstone for Fructuosus, who died at 18 years and 10 months, noting his piety and including the standard wish for light earth.

EDCS-05900018.text_edition: \n Manilius filius Man Turdulus PM nnorum XXC hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05900018.translation: Manilius, son of Manius Turdulus, aged 80, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05900018.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Manilius, son of Manius Turdulus, aged 80.

EDCS-05900047.text_edition: \n Quinto Fabio Sabino cae Sabini Fabiae Quinti filiae et ter laeta meo partu du addita sum exequis na (hei quae mater eram invida fors melius eis quam matris cultu nunc superi servat ereptosque dies mat et levibus terris m\n
EDCS-05900047.translation: To Quintus Fabius Sabinus, from Sabina, daughter of Quintus Fabius. I was joyful in my childbirth, yet I am added to the funeral rites. Oh, what an envious fate I had, that it was better for them to be away than to live under a mother's care. Now the gods preserve them, snatched away too soon, and the earth lightly upon them.
EDCS-05900047.summary: A funerary inscription, possibly for a mother mourning children or a child mourning a mother, lamenting premature death and the harshness of fate.

EDCS-05900050.text_edition: \n Danae nnorum XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05900050.translation: Danae lies here, aged 35. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05900050.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Danae, aged 35.

EDCS-05900051.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Antonia Terentia annorum XXXI caris sic suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05900051.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Antonia Terentia, aged 31, dear to her family, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05900051.summary: A funerary inscription for Antonia Terentia, aged 31, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-05900052.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Agathonicus annorum LXXV pius karus suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-05900052.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Agathonicus, aged 75, pious and dear to his family, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-05900052.summary: A funerary inscription for Agathonicus, aged 75, dedicated to the Manes.

EDCS-07000607.text_edition: \n Isis Murionumem sic tibi conmendo sic furtum meum mi fac tuto numini maiestati sic exsemplaria sic ut tu evides sic immedio qui fecit autulit sic aut heres opertorium album novum stragulum novum lodices duas meo uso sic rogo domina per maiestatem tuam ut hoc furtum reprindas sic\n
EDCS-07000607.translation: Isis of Murionum, I commend my stolen property to you, so that you may make my theft safe for me. By your divine majesty, by your exemplar and power, just as you see, I beg you, my lady, by your majesty, to recover this stolen property.
EDCS-07000607.summary: A votive inscription to the goddess Isis, asking for the recovery of stolen goods.

EDCS-07000618.text_edition: \n Hermes Heronis libertus rudis nnorum XX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07000618.translation: Hermes, freedman of Heron, newly freed, aged 20, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-07000618.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Hermes, a freedman of Heron, aged 20.

EDCS-07500010.text_edition: \n Caius Clodius Cai filius Galeria Blattianus nnorum XVIII huic ordo decrevit locum sepulturae turis pondo C statuam\n
EDCS-07500010.translation: Gaius Clodius Blattianus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, aged 18. The order decreed for him a burial place, 100 pounds of incense, and a statue.
EDCS-07500010.summary: A funerary inscription recording that the city council decreed a burial plot, incense, and a statue for Gaius Clodius Blattianus, aged 18.

EDCS-07500014.translation: Hermais lived 30 years, dear to his family, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-07500014.summary: A tombstone for Hermais, aged 30, noting he was dear to his family.

EDCS-07500015.translation: Mal() Sambaro, aged 50, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-07500015.summary: A tombstone for Mal() Sambaro, aged 50.

EDCS-07500022.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Sempronius Saturninus, aged forty-seven. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500022.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Sempronius Saturninus, who died at age 47, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500024.translation: Felicula, aged sixty, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500024.summary: A tombstone for Felicula, who died at age 60, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500026.translation: Lucius Siscinius Honora[tus], aged fifty-three, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500026.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Siscinius Honora[tus], who died at age 53, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500028.translation: Titus Flavius Aquila lived thirty years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Flavius Saturninus, his freedman, had this made for his well-deserving patron.
EDCS-07500028.summary: A tombstone for Titus Flavius Aquila, who died at age 30, with a dedication from his freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500029.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Annius Plautinus, from Baella, aged twenty-one, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500029.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Annius Plautinus, a native of Baella who died at age 21, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500032.translation: Cornelia Phoebas, mother, to Aemilia Optata. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500032.summary: A tombstone erected by Cornelia Phoebas for her daughter Aemilia Optata, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500046.text_edition: \n Quinto Pupio Urbico Galeria IIviro municipii Claudi Baelonensis ex decreto ordinis Quintus Pupius Genetivus pater et Iunia Eleuthera mater piissimo filio posuerunt\n
EDCS-07500046.translation: Quintus Pupius Genetivus, father, and Iunia Eleuthera, mother, dedicated this to their most dutiful son, Quintus Pupio Urbicus, IIvir of the municipality of Claudi Baelonensis, by decree of the council.
EDCS-07500046.summary: A funerary monument erected by parents for their son Quintus Pupio Urbicus, who held a local magistracy, in Claudi Baelonensis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500055.text_edition: \n Caius Canius Primulus annorum XXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07500055.translation: Caius Canius Primulus, aged thirty-five, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500055.summary: A tombstone for Caius Canius Primulus, who died at age 35, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500073.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Aemilius Adaes vixit nnos XLI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07500073.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Aemilius Adaes lived XLI years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500073.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Aemilius Adaes, who died at age 41, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500078.translation: Liberalis, a dear child to his family, aged five, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-07500078.summary: A tombstone for Liberalis, who died at age 5, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500166.translation: Lucius Firmius Salviculus, aged 23, dear to his family, is buried here. Lucius Firmius Urbanus, his father, [erected this] for his most dutiful and most indulgent son.
EDCS-07500166.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Firmius Salviculus, dedicated by his father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500195.text_edition: \n Marco Cornelio Luci filio Pusioni Theogenes libertus\n
EDCS-07500195.translation: Theogenes, freedman of Marcus Cornelius Pusion, son of Lucius.
EDCS-07500195.summary: A funerary epitaph for Theogenes, freedman of Marcus Cornelius Pusion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500243.text_edition: \n Caius Arrius Heraclida nnorum XXVI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Probatus frater bene merenti dedit\n
EDCS-07500243.translation: Gaius Arrius Heraclida, aged 26, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. His brother Probatus gave [this] to the deserving.
EDCS-07500243.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Arrius Heraclida, dedicated by his brother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500244.text_edition: \n Adventa cara suis us nnorum LXI sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-07500244.translation: Adventa, dear to her family, aged 61. May the earth lie lightly upon you all.
EDCS-07500244.summary: A funerary epitaph for Adventa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500245.text_edition: \n Quintus Iunius Saturninus annorum LXXX karus suis\n
EDCS-07500245.translation: Quintus Iunius Saturninus, aged 80, dear to his family.
EDCS-07500245.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Iunius Saturninus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500247.summary: A funerary epitaph simply naming Valeria Eleutera, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07500248.translation: Sacred to Victoria. Gaius Avielius Paelignus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, prefect for administering justice, made [this] at his own expense.
EDCS-07500248.summary: A dedication to the goddess Victoria by Gaius Avielius Paelignus, a local official, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600239.translation: Bracari, may you live with your family.
EDCS-07600239.summary: A wish for good health and prosperity to someone named Bracari, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800002.text_edition: \n Isidi Dominae Marcus Sempronius Maxumus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-07800002.translation: Marcus Sempronius Maxumus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Lady Isis.
EDCS-07800002.summary: A votive offering to the goddess Isis by Marcus Sempronius Maxumus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800003.text_edition: \n Isidi Dominae Lucius Vecilius libens animo votum solvit\n
EDCS-07800003.translation: Lucius Vecilius willingly fulfilled his vow to the Lady Isis.
EDCS-07800003.summary: A votive offering to the goddess Isis by Lucius Vecilius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800023.text_edition: \n Lucius Fabius Novellus nnorum LXIIX Plotia Septuma sic mater fecit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-07800023.translation: Lucius Fabius Novellus, aged 68. Plotia Septuma, his mother, made this. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07800023.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Fabius Novellus, erected by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800028.translation: Siscinia Novata, daughter of Quintus, aged 38, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-07800028.summary: A funerary epitaph for Siscinia Novata, an adult woman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07800031.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Vibia Iuliana is buried here.
EDCS-07800031.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vibia Iuliana, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300402.text_edition: \n Lucio Pontio Cai filio Sergia Amoena filia\n
EDCS-08300402.translation: To Lucius Pontius, son of Caius. From his daughter Sergia Amoena.
EDCS-08300402.summary: An epitaph or dedication for Lucius Pontius, erected by his daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300405.translation: Emeritus, his freedman, gives this to our Titus.
EDCS-08300405.summary: A dedication or funerary marker, possibly for a patron, erected by a freedman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08400309.text_edition: \n Quintus C C deae Invictae sacrum\n
EDCS-08400309.translation: Quintus dedicates this sacred offering to the Invicta goddess.
EDCS-08400309.summary: A religious dedication to the goddess Invicta by Quintus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500422.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sergia Rustica nnorum LXXXX pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08500422.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sergia Rustica, aged 90, dutiful towards her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08500422.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sergia Rustica, an elderly woman, erected by herself or her family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600350.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Sertorius Maternus annorum XXVII\n
EDCS-08600350.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Marcus Sertorius Maternus, aged 27.
EDCS-08600350.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Sertorius Maternus, a young man, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600351.text_edition: \n Epaprhoditus sic Babis hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600351.translation: Epaprhoditus is buried here. From Babis.
EDCS-08600351.summary: A funerary epitaph for Epaprhoditus, erected by Babis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600352.text_edition: \n Marci Clodi Rufini annorum XXIII ab sic latronibus occisus est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08600352.translation: Killed by brigands. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600352.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the violent death of Marci Clodi Rufini, aged 23, by brigands, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600356.text_edition: \n Terrenum corpus caelestis spiritus in me quo repente suam sedem nunc vivimus illic et fruitur superis aeterna in luce Fabatus\n
EDCS-08600356.translation: The earthly body and the heavenly spirit found a home in me, so that we now live and enjoy the eternal light of the heavens, Fabatus.
EDCS-08600356.summary: This inscription is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely dating to the imperial period, recording the spirit and earthly remains of Fabatus.

EDCS-08600358.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely from Roman Italy, dating to the Imperial period, recording a filiation.

EDCS-08600359.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely funerary, from an unknown location, dating to the Imperial period, commemorating a man and his parent.

EDCS-08600360.text_edition: \n Imperatoris Cai Iuli Veri Maximini provinciae \n
EDCS-08600360.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Gnaeus Cornelius Capito, councilor of Mentesa, aged 43. Pious in his own affairs, he inscribed in his will that this monument be made for himself by his freedmen, Cornelius Floridus, Italianus, and Septimius. Lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08600360.summary: This is a funerary monument from Mentesa (modern Montilla, Spain), likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE, for a local councilor, commissioned by his freedmen according to his will.

EDCS-08600361.text_edition: \n Minervae Augustae sacrum M P V\n
EDCS-08600361.translation: Sacred to Minerva Augusta. M. P. V.
EDCS-08600361.summary: This is a dedication, likely from Roman Italy dating to the Imperial period, to the goddess Minerva Augusta.

EDCS-08600363.text_edition: \n Euanthus annorum XXIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-08600363.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely dating to the Imperial period, recording the name and age of the deceased.

EDCS-08700003.text_edition: \n Vestae Augustae sacrum Tiberius Claudius Felix Tiberi Claudi Fortunati libertus accepto loco ab ordine Mentesanorum ob honorem VIviratus de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700003.translation: Sacred to Vesta Augusta. Tiberius Claudius Felix, freedman of Tiberius Claudius Fortunatus, having received a plot of land from the council of Mentesa, dedicated and donated it at his own expense for the honor of the office of VIvir.
EDCS-08700003.summary: This is a dedication from Mentesa (modern Montilla, Spain), likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE, by a freedman to the goddess Vesta Augusta, commemorating his public office.

EDCS-08700004.text_edition: \n Agrippinae Cai Caesaris Augusti Germanici matri Quintus Fabius Hispanus flamen Augusti decreto ordinis dedit\n
EDCS-08700004.translation: To Agrippina, mother of Emperor Caius Caesar Augustus Germanicus. Quintus Fabius Hispanus, priest of Augustus, dedicated it by decree of the council.
EDCS-08700004.summary: This is a dedication from an unknown location, likely dating to the 1st century CE, to Agrippina the Elder, by a priest of Augustus.

EDCS-08700005.text_edition: \n Gaius Iulius Primus miles veteranus legionis X geminae hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700005.translation: Gaius Julius Primus, veteran soldier of the Legio X Gemina, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700005.summary: This is a funerary epitaph from an unknown location, likely dating to the Imperial period, for a veteran soldier.

EDCS-08700006.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cnaeo Cornelio Capitoni decurioni Mentesano nnum XLIII pius in suis monimentum sibi fieri testamento suo adscripsit ab Corneliis Florido Italiciano Septimio libertis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700006.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Gnaeus Cornelius Capito, a councilor from Mentesa, aged 43, pious in his own way, directed in his will that this monument be made for himself. Italiciano Septimio, freedman of the Cornelii, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700006.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gnaeus Cornelius Capito, a councilor from Mentesa, who died at age 43 and who stipulated in his will that this monument be erected for him.

EDCS-08700010.text_edition: \n Valeriae Fortunatae Lucius Fabius Glycon uxori dedit dedicavit accepto loco ab ordine MeMentesano\n
EDCS-08700010.translation: Lucius Fabius Glycon gave and dedicated this to his wife Valeria Fortunata, having received the location from the order of the MeMentesani.
EDCS-08700010.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Fabius Glycon to his wife Valeria Fortunata, with land provided by the MeMentesani order, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700011.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Baebia Faustiana Ilubariensia annorum LVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700011.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Baebia Faustiana of Iluberi, aged 58, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700011.summary: A tombstone inscription for Baebia Faustiana, aged 58, likely from the Roman Imperial period, invoking a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-08700012.text_edition: \n Aelia Luci liberta Badia annorum XXXIIII pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Fabius Fortunatus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08700012.translation: Badia, freedwoman of Lucius Aelius, aged 34, dutiful to her own people, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you. Fabius Fortunatus arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-08700012.summary: A tombstone inscription for Badia, a freedwoman, aged 34, arranged by Fabius Fortunatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700013.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelia Orchis annorum LVII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700013.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Cornelia Orchis, aged 57, dutiful to her own people, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700013.summary: A tombstone inscription for Cornelia Orchis, aged 57, likely from the Roman Imperial period, invoking a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-08700015.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum F Graphicus V F T annorum X pius in suis hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700015.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. F. Graphicus, son of V. T., aged 10, dutiful to his own people, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700015.summary: A tombstone inscription for a young boy named F. Graphicus, aged 10, likely from the Roman Imperial period, invoking a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-08700016.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Q sacrum Iulia annorum XX pia in suis hic sita est\n
EDCS-08700016.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Julia, aged 20, dutiful to her own people, is buried here.
EDCS-08700016.summary: A tombstone inscription for Julia, aged 20, likely from the Roman Imperial period, stating her burial and dutifulness.

EDCS-08700017.summary: This is a name inscription, likely commemorating Marcus Poplicius Fuscinus, possibly on a tomb or public monument, from the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-08700018.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum ius Remus AS AOS \n
EDCS-08700018.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. ius Remus AS AOS
EDCS-08700018.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to the Spirits of the Departed, with illegible text following, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700020.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Valerius Asiaticus nnorum XXIX pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700020.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Valerius Asiaticus, aged 29, dutiful to his own people, is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700020.summary: A tombstone inscription for Quintus Valerius Asiaticus, aged 29, likely from the Roman Imperial period, wishing him a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-08700021.text_edition: \n Quintus Valerius Miro hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700021.translation: Quintus Valerius Miro is buried here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08700021.summary: A tombstone inscription for Quintus Valerius Miro, likely from the Roman Imperial period, wishing him a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-08700022.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Valerianus annorum XII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700022.translation: Marcus Valerius Valerianus, aged 12, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700022.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Valerius Valerianus, a young boy, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700023.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valeria Nice uxor Cornelio Firmano marito nnum LXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700023.translation: To the departed spirits. Valeria Nice, wife of Cornelius Firman, aged 61, devoted to her own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700023.summary: A tombstone for Valeria Nice, wife of Cornelius Firman, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700027.text_edition: \n Apollini Augusto Quintus Annius Quinti Anni Aproniani libertus Gallus Patriciensis ob honorem VIviratus sui adiutorio Quinti Anni Fabiani fili sui dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700027.translation: To Apollo Augustus. Gallus, freedman of Quintus Annius Gallus Apronianus, presented and dedicated this in honor of his office as a VIvir, with the assistance of his son Quintus Annius Fabianus.
EDCS-08700027.summary: A dedication to Apollo Augustus by a freedman in honor of his magistracy, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700028.text_edition: \n Dis deabusque sacrum Cornelius Aprilis libens animo solvit lyricarius\n
EDCS-08700028.translation: Sacred to the gods and goddesses. Cornelius Aprilis, a lyre player, willingly fulfilled this obligation.
EDCS-08700028.summary: A religious dedication by Cornelius Aprilis, a lyre player, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Fabius Probus Aurgitanus flamen municipii Flavii Aurgitani pontifex perpetuus Div August- nnorum XXXVIII pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700031.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Fabius Probus of Aurgitano, priest of the municipality of Flavius Aurgitanus and perpetual pontiff of Divus Augustus, aged 38, devoted to his own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700031.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Fabius Probus, an important local official in Aurgitano, who died at age 38, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700032.text_edition: \n Caius Sempronius Cai filius Galeria Sempronianus IIvir bis pontufex perpetuus et Sempronia Fusca Vibia Anicilla filia thermas aqua perducta cum silvis agnuarum trecentarum pecunia impensaque sua omni dederunt dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-08700032.translation: Gaius Sempronius Sempronianus, son of Gaius, of the Galeria tribe, twice IIvir and perpetual pontiff, and Sempronia Fusca Vibia Anicilla, daughter, gave and dedicated the baths with the aqueduct and 300 units of land with their woods, at their own expense and cost.
EDCS-08700032.summary: A public works dedication of baths and an aqueduct by two prominent local officials, Gaius Sempronius Sempronianus and Sempronia Fusca Vibia Anicilla, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700033.text_edition: \n Lucius Manilius Gallus et Lucius Manilius Alexander Aurgitani ob honorem VI<vi>ratus secundum petitionem municipum municipii optimi patroni loca spectaculorum numero CC singuli ex duplici pecunia decreto optimi ordinis municipibus municipii Aurgitani dederunt donaverunt\n
EDCS-08700033.translation: Lucius Manilius Gallus and Lucius Manilius Alexander of Aurgitano, patrons of the most excellent municipality, gave and donated 200 places for spectacles to the townspeople of the most excellent municipality of Aurgitano, from double the amount of money, according to the decree of the most excellent council, in honor of their second VIviratus office, following the petition of the townspeople.
EDCS-08700033.summary: A donation of public entertainment venues by Lucius Manilius Gallus and Lucius Manilius Alexander to the municipality of Aurgitano, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700034.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Fabius Florinus Aurgitanus VIvir municipii Flavi Aurgitani nnum LXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700034.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Fabius Florinus of Aurgitano, a VIvir of the Flavius Aurgitanus municipality, aged 70, devoted to his own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700034.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Fabius Florinus, a local official in Aurgitano, who died at age 70, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700035.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus M Venustus sevir nnorum XXII \n
EDCS-08700035.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus M. Venustus, a sevir, aged 22.
EDCS-08700035.summary: A tombstone fragment for Marcus M. Venustus, a sevir, who died at age 22, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700036.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Valerius Attictus Aurgitanus sevir annorum LXV pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700036.translation: To the departed spirits. Valerius Attictus of Aurgitano, a sevir, aged 65, devoted to his own people. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08700036.summary: A tombstone for Valerius Attictus of Aurgitano, a sevir, who died at age 65, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08700038.text_edition: \n Cosanae Cai filiae Fidentinae matronae post mortem\n
EDCS-08700038.translation: To Cosana, daughter of Caius, a matron from Fidentia, after her death.
EDCS-08700038.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a deceased woman named Cosana.

EDCS-08700039.text_edition: \n Fabiae Threpti Murria Phiale dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700039.translation: Murria Phiale gave and dedicated this to Fabia Threptus.
EDCS-08700039.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Murria Phiale to Fabia Threptus.

EDCS-08700040.translation: To Mamilia, by decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-08700040.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Mamilia, enacted by a decree of the town councillors.

EDCS-08700041.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Annius Quinti libertus Annianus Patriciensis annorum XX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700041.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Annius Annianus, son of Quintus, freedman, from Patricienses, aged 20, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700041.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Annius Annianus, who died at the age of 20, recording his piety and burial place.

EDCS-08700042.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Annius Felix Aurgitanus nnum LXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700042.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Annius Felix, from Aurgita, aged 75, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700042.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Annius Felix, who died at the age of 75, recording his piety and burial place.

EDCS-08700043.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Cassiusus Tuccitanus incola Aurgitanus annorum hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700043.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Quintus Cassius Tuccitanus, inhabitant from Aurgita, aged [---], is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700043.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Cassius Tuccitanus, an inhabitant from Aurgita, recording his burial place.

EDCS-08700044.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulius Gemellus Astigi annorum LXXX pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700044.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius Gemellus, from Astigi, aged 80, dutiful to his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700044.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Iulius Gemellus, who died at the age of 80, recording his piety and burial place.

EDCS-08700045.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Manilius Fortunatus Aurgitanus annorum XVIII pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700045.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lucius Manilius Fortunatus, from Aurgita, aged 18, dutiful to his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700045.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Manilius Fortunatus, who died at the age of 18, recording his piety and burial place.

EDCS-08700046.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Fulvia Cnaei filia Proba nnum LXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700046.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Fulvia Proba, daughter of Cnaeus, aged 75, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700046.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fulvia Proba, who died at the age of 75, recording her burial place.

EDCS-08700047.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum ae Auli filiae Lupercillae nnum XXXXVIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700047.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. [---], daughter of Aulus, Lupercilla, aged 48, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700047.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lupercilla, daughter of Aulus, who died at the age of 48, recording her piety and burial place.

EDCS-08700048.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Octavia Gai filia Honesta annorurum LVI mensium V di<e>bus XX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700048.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Octavia Honest a, daughter of Gaius, fifty-six years, five months, and twenty days old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700048.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavia Honesta, who died at the age of 56 years, 5 months, and 20 days.

EDCS-08700051.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Publi filio Galeria Urso IIviro municipum municipii Flavi Aurgitani decreto decurionum Acilia Ploce mater honorem accepit inpensam remisit\n
EDCS-08700051.translation: Acilia Ploce, mother, received the honor for Publius Aelius Urso, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir of the municipium of Flavius Aurgitanus, by decree of the decurions, and remitted the expense.
EDCS-08700051.summary: An epitaph recording an honor granted to the deceased Publius Aelius Urso, a duovir, which his mother Acilia Ploce accepted and paid for.

EDCS-08700052.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus invocatio et memoria Quinti Iuli Deuteri \n
EDCS-08700052.translation: Invocation and memorial to Quintus Iulius Deuteros.
EDCS-08700052.summary: A memorial inscription for Quintus Iulius Deuteros.

EDCS-08700053.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Gai liberta Genesis annorum XXXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700053.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Genesis, freedwoman of Gaius, forty-five years old, pious, lies here among her own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700053.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Genesis, a freedwoman, who died at the age of 45.

EDCS-08700057.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Ennius Nomin et Aemilia Augustina Quinto Ennio Iuveni filio karissimo et dulcissimo nobis anniculo et dierum VIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08700057.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Ennius Nomin and Aemilia Augustina placed here their dearest and sweetest son Quintus Ennius Iuvenis, one year old and eight days. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08700057.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Ennius Iuvenis, son of Quintus Ennius Nomin and Aemilia Augustina, who died at one year and eight days of age.

EDCS-08700058.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum domino bene merenti mater et pater filio posuerunt qui defunctus est Remus in trimatu\n
EDCS-08700058.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For a well-deserving master, his mother and father placed this for their son who died in his third year.
EDCS-08700058.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by parents for their son, who died in his third year.

EDCS-08700066.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius V M votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08700066.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Publius V.M. fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08700066.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-08700067.text_edition: \n M ronia Marci filia Saturnina de su- \n
EDCS-08700067.summary: An incomplete inscription that appears to be the beginning of a dedication or funerary record for Mronia Saturnina.

EDCS-08700068.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto pontifici maximo Armeniaco Parthico Maximo Medico tribuniciae potestatis XX imperatori V consuli III patri patriae divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti conservatori generis humani ob honorem pontificatus Marci Sergii Materni mariti Annia Quinti filia Severa epulo diviso editis circensibus posuit et dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700068.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, conqueror of Armenia, Parthia, and Media, holder of tribunician power for the 20th time, Imperator for the 5th time, Consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland, son of the divine Antoninus, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, descendant of the divine Nerva, preserver of the human race, on the occasion of his pontificate, Marcus Sergius Maternus, husband of Annia Severa, daughter of Quintus, dedicated a feast and games. Annia Severa erected and dedicated this.
EDCS-08700068.summary: An inscription honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, dedicated by Annia Severa in celebration of his pontificate, which included a feast and games, likely dating to his 20th tribunician power term and 5th acclamation as Imperator.

EDCS-08700069.text_edition: \n Publio Fabio Publi filio Galeria Iuliano IIviro bis pontifici ordo Batorensis decrevit Iunia Marci filia Severa mater honore usa impensam remissit sic\n
EDCS-08700069.translation: The order of the Batorenses decreed to Publius Fabius Iulianus, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, twice duovir and twice pontiff. Iunia Severa, daughter of Marcus, his mother, used the honor and remitted the expense, as follows:
EDCS-08700069.summary: A record of an honor decreed by the town council of Batorensis to Publius Fabius Iulianus, a prominent local official, which his mother Iunia Severa funded.

EDCS-08700072.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman named Verana, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700075.text_edition: \n Herculi Invicto Tiberius Iulius Augusti filius Divi nepos Caesar Augustus imperator pontifex maxumus sic dedit [Quintus Granius Quinti filius Marcianus proconsul] [faciendum curavit idemque dedicavit]\n
EDCS-08700075.translation: To Hercules the Unconquered, Tiberius Julius Caesar Augustus Imperator, son of Augustus, grandson of the Divine [Caesar], chief priest; thus dedicated [Quintus Granius Marcianus, son of Quintus, proconsul] arranged for it to be made and dedicated it himself.
EDCS-08700075.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Hercules Invictus by Emperor Tiberius Julius Caesar Augustus, with a proconsul named Quintus Granius Marcianus overseeing its construction and dedication, dating to the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700076.text_edition: \n Herculi Cnaeus Annaeus Natalis Patriciensis posuit\n
EDCS-08700076.translation: Cnaeus Annaeus Natalis of Patricius erected [this] to Hercules.
EDCS-08700076.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument to Hercules by Cnaeus Annaeus Natalis, likely a private dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700079.text_edition: \n Pietati Augustae Lucius Lucretius Fulvianus flamen coloniarum immunium provinciae Baeticae pontifex perpetuus domus Augustae testamento poni iussit ex argenti pondo ob honorem pontificatus Lucretia Luci filia Campana flaminica perpetua domus Augustae editis ad dedicationem scaenicis ludis per quadriduum et circensibus et epulo diviso posuit huic dono Lucretia Campana amplius nomine suo coronam auream adiunxit donum dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08700079.translation: To Augustan Piety, Lucius Lucretius Fulvianus, flamen of the immune colonies of the province of Baetica, perpetual priest of the Augustan household, ordered by his will to be set up from a silver pound for the honor of his priesthood; Lucretia Campana, daughter of Lucius, perpetual priestess of the Augustan household, having presented stage games for four days and circus games and a shared feast for the dedication, set [it] up; Lucretia Campana added to this gift a golden crown in her own name, gave, and dedicated [it].
EDCS-08700079.summary: This complex inscription details a dedication to Augustan Piety by Lucius Lucretius Fulvianus, a high-ranking priest, and his wife Lucretia Campana, including the funding, the lavish ceremonies for its dedication, and an additional gift by Lucretia, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08700080.text_edition: \n Cassia Luci filia Quarta votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08700080.translation: Quarta, daughter of Lucius Cassius, fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-08700080.summary: This is a votive inscription by Quarta, daughter of Lucius Cassius, fulfilling a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700082.text_edition: \n Augusto Quintus Iulius Quinti filius Sergia Celsus aedilis IIvir \n
EDCS-08700082.translation: To Augustus, Quintus Julius Celsus, son of Quintus, of the Sergia tribe, as aedile and twice duovir.
EDCS-08700082.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the divine Augustus by Quintus Julius Celsus, who held the offices of aedile and duovir, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08700083.translation: To Alfidia, mother of the Augustan family.
EDCS-08700083.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Alfidia, referred to as "mother of the Augustan family", possibly honoring a prominent matron, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08700084.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi pacatoris orbis filio decreto decurionum res publica Tuccitanorum\n
EDCS-08700084.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, pacifier of the world, the public community of Tuccita, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08700084.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by the community of Tuccita to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla), son of Septimius Severus, by decree of the local council, dating to his reign (198-217 CE).

EDCS-08700085.text_edition: \n Publio Septimi[o Getae nobilissimo] Caesari Luci Septimii Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi pacatoris orbis filio et Marci Aureli Antonini imperatoris fratri res publica Tuccitanorum datum decreto decurionum\n
