EDCS-08701083.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Calpurnia Restituta, recording her age and piety, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701084.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Carvilius Alcibiades nnum XXVII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701084.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Carvilius Alcibiades, 27 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701084.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Carvilius Alcibiades, recording his age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701085.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Chresimus annorum XXXI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701085.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Chresimus, 31 years old, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701085.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Chresimus, recording his age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701086.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelius Apollonius vixit nnos XLI menses II pius in suis sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08701086.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Apollonius lived 41 years and 2 months, dutiful to his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701086.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Apollonius, recording his lifespan and piety, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701087.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cornelius Rest<it>utus vixit nnos numero LXXXXI dies V pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701087.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelius Restitutus lived 91 years and 5 days, dutiful to his family. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701087.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Restitutus, recording his lifespan and piety, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701088.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, mentioning someone's piety towards their family.

EDCS-08701091.summary: This inscription likely refers to Gnaeus Pompeius Magnus (Pompey the Great), possibly marking a monument or honorific, dating to the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08701094.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a boy named Primus, recording his age, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701097.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Deuterus Semproniae -- nnorum XXV? hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701097.translation: Lucius Valerius Deuterus [buried] Sempronia -- , 25 years old. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701097.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sempronia, likely dedicated by Lucius Valerius Deuterus, recording her age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701100.text_edition: \n Apollini Augusto Vibia Trophime votum animo libens solvit\n
EDCS-08701100.translation: Vibia Trophime willingly fulfilled her vow to Apollo Augustus.
EDCS-08701100.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Vibia Trophime fulfilling a vow to Apollo Augustus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701102.text_edition: \n Caius Vettius Cai filius Sergia centurio legionis XXX IIvir iterum Genio coloniae Genetivae Iuliae sacrum dat\n
EDCS-08701102.translation: Gaius Vettius, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, centurion of the 30th Legion, twice chief magistrate, dedicates this sacred offering to the Genius of the colony of Genetiva Julia.
EDCS-08701102.summary: A dedication to the Genius of the colony by a high-ranking military and civic official, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-08701106.text_edition: \n ------] -RIT Aelia Apra Tispitana sacer<d=O>os perpetu<a=V> <vix=A>it annis XXXV mensibus V dies sic XXVIII pia in <s>(uis) <h>(ic) <s>(ita) <e>(st) <s>(it) <t>(ibi) <t>(erra) <l>(evis) \n
EDCS-08701106.translation: Aelia Apra Tispitana, perpetual priestess, lived 35 years, 5 months, and 28 days; may the earth lie lightly on you, placed here.
EDCS-08701106.summary: A tombstone inscription for a woman who held a lifelong priestly office, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701107.text_edition: \n Caius Aemilius Cai filius Sergia Faustinus annorum XVIIII hic situs est huic decuriones Genetivi decreverunt ornamenta IIviralia inpensam funeris locum sepulturae lapides ad monimentum\n
EDCS-08701107.translation: Gaius Aemilius Faustinus, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, aged 19, is buried here; for him the decurions of Genetiva decreed the powers of a chief magistrate, the expenses for the funeral, a burial plot, and stones for the monument.
EDCS-08701107.summary: A decree by the local council honoring a young man with a public funeral and monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701108.text_edition: \n io Luci filio Galeria Gallo praefecto fabrorum avo et Rufae aviae nepos faciendum curavit sit? vobis? terra? levis?\n
EDCS-08701108.translation: His grandson arranged for this to be made for Lucius, son of Gaius Gal(l)us, prefect of the engineers, his grandfather, and for Rufa, his grandmother; may the earth lie lightly on you?
EDCS-08701108.summary: A funerary monument erected by a grandson for his grandparents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701109.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Marci filio Sergia Sabino IIviro pontifici perpetuo colonorum coloniae Genetivae Iuliae\n
EDCS-08701109.translation: To Marcus Valerius Sabinus, son of Marcus, of the Sergia tribe, twice chief magistrate, perpetual priest of the colonists of the colony of Genetiva Julia.
EDCS-08701109.summary: An honorific inscription for a prominent local official and priest, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701112.text_edition: \n Marcus Aemilienus filius decurionum faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08701112.translation: Marcus Aemilienus, son of the decurions, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-08701112.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by an individual associated with the local council, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701113.text_edition: \n Decimus Cornelius Virgulei Eutychi filius Verus restituit\n
EDCS-08701113.translation: Decimus Cornelius Verus, son of Virguleius Eutychus, restored this.
EDCS-08701113.summary: An inscription recording the restoration of a monument by an individual, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701115.text_edition: \n Caio Aemilio Graccuri A ob merita \n
EDCS-08701115.translation: To Gaius Aemilius Gracchus. For his merits.
EDCS-08701115.summary: An honorific inscription acknowledging the merits of Gaius Aemilius Gracchus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701119.text_edition: \n Aelia mulieris liberta Firmis annorum XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701119.translation: Aelia Firmis, freedwoman of Mulier, aged 35, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701119.summary: A tombstone inscription for a freedwoman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701120.text_edition: \n Amerina Cai filia Anulla Aelii Hectoris nnum LXXVI hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701120.translation: Anulla, daughter of Gaius, of the Amerina tribe, wife of Aelius Hector, aged 76, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701120.summary: A tombstone inscription for a woman identifying her paternal tribe and her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701121.text_edition: \n Argentaria Luci filia Herois annorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701121.translation: Herois, daughter of Lucius, the silversmith, aged 25, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701121.summary: A funerary epitaph for Herois, a silversmith, who died at age 25.

EDCS-08701122.text_edition: \n Argentaria Luci liberta Eucherea sibi et Lucio Argentario Pamphilo viro suo et patrono\n
EDCS-08701122.translation: Eucherea, freedwoman of Lucius, for herself and for Lucius Argentarius Pamphilus, her husband and patron.
EDCS-08701122.summary: An epitaph for Eucherea, a freedwoman, who made provision for herself and her husband/patron, Lucius Argentarius Pamphilus.

EDCS-08701123.summary: A brief epitaph for Caius Optandus, who died at age 15.

EDCS-08701124.text_edition: \n Linia sic Attiae Hedone loca III\n
EDCS-08701124.translation: Linia Hedone, for Attius, three plots of land.
EDCS-08701124.summary: A record of property allocation, where Linia Hedone assigned three plots to Attius.

EDCS-08701126.text_edition: \n Caesia Cai filia Sperata nnorum XIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XV\n
EDCS-08701126.translation: Caesia Sperata, daughter of Caius, aged 13, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. 12 feet in front, 15 feet in the field.
EDCS-08701126.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caesia Sperata, aged 13, including details of the plot size.

EDCS-08701128.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus sacrum Camilla mater filio suo Licinio fecit sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701128.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Camilla made this for her son Licinius. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701128.summary: A funerary dedication by Camilla to her son Licinius.

EDCS-08701129.text_edition: \n <Ti=LE>(berius) <C=L>laudius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701129.translation: Tiberius Claudius is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701129.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius.

EDCS-08701132.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Doro Pothine coniugi annorum plus minus LXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701132.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Doro for Pothine, his wife, aged about 65, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701132.summary: A funerary dedication by Doro for his wife Pothine, who died around age 65.

EDCS-08701133.text_edition: \n Cai libertus Faustus XX nnorum sic hic situs est sit tibi terra levis immatura tui properantur tempora fati primaque praecipiti limine vita ruit viginti tecum nam fers non amplius annos sed decuit talem longior hora virum\n
EDCS-08701133.translation: Faustus, freedman of Caius, aged 20, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. The times of fate hasten immaturely, and life rushes away from the first threshold; for you bear not more than twenty years, but such a man deserved a longer hour.
EDCS-08701133.summary: A funerary epitaph for Faustus, a freedman aged 20, with a poetic lament on his premature death.

EDCS-08701136.translation: Grania Helpaesusa, freedwoman, three plots of land.
EDCS-08701136.summary: A record of property allocation, where Grania Helpaesusa, a freedwoman, granted three plots of land.

EDCS-08701137.translation: Three plots for Iulia Moschis' freedwoman.
EDCS-08701137.summary: This inscription records the allocation of burial plots, likely in a cemetery, to a freedwoman of Iulia Moschis.

EDCS-08701139.text_edition: \n hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis a Lucana annorum \n
EDCS-08701139.translation: Here she is buried; may the earth lie lightly on you, Lucana, aged [---].
EDCS-08701139.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucana, with a common epitaph wishing her peace.

EDCS-08701140.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marciana nnorum XXXXVII pia in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701140.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marciana, aged 47, pious, with her family. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701140.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marciana, aged 47, and includes a standard epitaph wishing her peace.

EDCS-08701141.text_edition: \n Murtale nnorum X hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701141.translation: Murtale, aged 10, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701141.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Murtale, a child aged 10, with a standard epitaph.

EDCS-08701143.text_edition: \n Optatus Calpurniae Vernae filius vixit nnis XVIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701143.translation: Optatus, son of Calpurnia Vernas, lived 18 years, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701143.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Optatus, son of Calpurnia Vernas, who lived 18 years.

EDCS-08701144.text_edition: \n Caius Papirius Cai filius Cornelia Iuniae Marci filiae matri Papiriae Cai filiae filiai sic\n
EDCS-08701144.translation: Gaius Papirius, son of Caius, to his mother Papiria, daughter of Caius, daughter of Cornelia Iunia, daughter of Marcus, his daughter.
EDCS-08701144.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial, possibly from a son to his mother and other female relatives, detailing their lineage.

EDCS-08701145.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely the beginning of a name or names, possibly from a funerary or dedication monument.

EDCS-08701146.text_edition: \n Sabidia annorum LV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701146.translation: Sabidia, aged 55, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701146.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Sabidia, aged 55, with a standard epitaph.

EDCS-08701148.text_edition: \n Titus Talenus Titi filius Pudens R annorum XXXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701148.translation: Titus Talenus Pudens, son of Titus, aged 32, is buried here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701148.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Talenus Pudens, son of Titus, who was 32 years old.

EDCS-08701149.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, Thallio.
EDCS-08701149.summary: This brief inscription is a dedication to the departed spirits (Dis Manibus), likely for a tomb or grave marker.

EDCS-08701153.text_edition: \n Valeria Luci filia Optata mensium XI et dierum XXIII hic sita est dic rogo praeteriens sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701153.translation: Valeria, daughter of Lucius, aged eleven months and twenty-three days, lies here. Please, passerby, say: 'May the earth lie lightly on you.'
EDCS-08701153.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of a young child, Valeria, with a common plea to passersby.

EDCS-08701154.translation: Adis Linea, daughter of Vi Firmus, aged [?].
EDCS-08701154.summary: A fragmentary epitaph possibly recording the name and age of a deceased individual, Adis Linea.

EDCS-08701158.text_edition: \n s Cai filius sibi patri matri \n
EDCS-08701158.translation: S., son of Caius, for himself, his father, and his mother.
EDCS-08701158.summary: A funerary inscription fragment mentioning a person named S. dedicating the monument for his family.

EDCS-08701161.text_edition: \n F V BETE CA ia Quinti filia IgI H \n
EDCS-08701161.translation: F. V. Bete, daughter of Quintus, IgI H.
EDCS-08701161.summary: A very fragmentary inscription possibly naming a woman, Bete, and her father Quintus, with unclear additional abbreviations.

EDCS-08701184.summary: A name likely from a sepulchral monument or a tombstone, identifying Aemilius Severus.

EDCS-08701189.text_edition: \n Arbori sanctae Quintus Avidius Augustinus ex visu posuit\n
EDCS-08701189.translation: Quintus Avidius Augustinus erected this for the sacred tree, after seeing it in a vision.
EDCS-08701189.summary: A dedication to a sacred tree by Quintus Avidius Augustinus, who was prompted by a vision.

EDCS-08701190.text_edition: \n Lucio Sergio Regis filio Arnensi Plauto quaestori Salio Palatino patrono\n
EDCS-08701190.translation: To Lucius Sergius Regillus, son of Primus, of the Arnensis tribe, Plautus, Salii Palatini, quaestor and patron.
EDCS-08701190.summary: A dedication honoring Lucius Sergius Regillus, specifying his tribal affiliation, military rank (quaestor), and association with the Salii Palatini.

EDCS-08701193.text_edition: \n Quinto Rutilio Publi filio Galeria Flacco Corneliano tribuno militum legionis VIII Augustae\n
EDCS-08701193.translation: To Quintus Rutilius Flaccus, son of Publius, Cornelianus, tribune of the soldiers of the Eighth Legion Augusta.
EDCS-08701193.summary: A military dedication to Quintus Rutilius Flaccus, identifying his father, his tribe, and his rank as tribune in the Eighth Legion Augusta.

EDCS-08701194.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gaius Numisius Titicus Tispitanus nnrum XXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701194.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Gaius Numisius Titicus, of Tispitanus, aged thirty years, pious in his own home, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701194.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Numisius Titicus, recording his age, piety, and location of burial, with a customary wish for the deceased.

EDCS-08701195.text_edition: \n Fabia Ianuaria nnorum XXV pia in suis quisquis tunc vivis amasti si modo sunt noctes nec periere dies sit tibi terra levis posuit Fabius Venustus patronus\n
EDCS-08701195.translation: Fabia Ianuaria, aged twenty-five years, pious in her own home. Whoever among the living you loved, if only the nights exist and days have not passed, may the earth lie lightly on you. Fabius Venustus, the patron, placed this.
EDCS-08701195.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabia Ianuaria, noting her age and piety, with an emotional appeal and a dedication by her patron, Fabius Venustus.

EDCS-08701200.summary: This inscription likely records an honorific title or dedication related to an emperor named Gnaeus Magnus, possibly from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08701202.text_edition: \n Briseis nutrix Quinti Rutili Quinti filii Flacci Corneliani annorum XXXV pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701202.translation: Briseis, nurse of Quintus Rutilius Flaccus, son of Quintus Cornelianus, aged 35, dutiful to her family, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701202.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Briseis, the nurse of Quintus Rutilius Flaccus Cornelianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701203.text_edition: \n Afrae nostrae Turpio libertus dono dedit et Vespicia D\n
EDCS-08701203.translation: Turpio, freedman, gave as a gift our Afra and Vespicia D[...].
EDCS-08701203.summary: This inscription records a dedication or gift made by Turpio, a freedman, to his freedwoman Afra and Vespicia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701205.text_edition: \n Valeria Gai filia nnum X hic sita est dic qui legis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701205.translation: Valeria, daughter of Gaius, aged 10, is laid here; say, you who read this, may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701205.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Valeria, daughter of Gaius, who died at the age of 10, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701206.text_edition: \n Marcus Porcius Marci filius Galeria Gallicus annorum XLV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701206.translation: Marcus Porcius Gallicus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aged 45, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701206.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Marcus Porcius Gallicus, aged 45, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701208.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Hesiodus et Licinia Homulla hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-08701208.translation: Marcus Ulpius Hesiodus and Licinia Homulla are laid here.
EDCS-08701208.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the burial of Marcus Ulpius Hesiodus and Licinia Homulla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701211.summary: This fragmentary inscription mentions a person from Obulcula (?) who was 70 years old, likely a tombstone from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701214.text_edition: \n Capito Sunnae filius decurio equitum alae geminae legionis X Rustica Galli filia\n
EDCS-08701214.translation: To Gaius Sunna, son of Capito, decurion of the cavalry of the Twin Wing of the Tenth Legion, and Rustica, daughter of Gallus.
EDCS-08701214.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or honorific text for Capito Sunna, a decurion in a Roman legion, and his wife Rustica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701215.text_edition: \n Caio Aelio Cai filio Turpioni mater\n
EDCS-08701215.translation: His mother to Gaius Aelius Turpio, son of Caius.
EDCS-08701215.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a mother to her son, Gaius Aelius Turpio, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701216.text_edition: \n Antiopa Diocharis liberta annorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701216.translation: Antiopa, freedwoman of Diocharis, aged 50, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701216.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Antiopa, freedwoman of Diocharis, who died at the age of 50, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701217.text_edition: \n Decia mulieris liberta Felicula annorum XXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701217.translation: Felicula, freedwoman of Decia, aged 22, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701217.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicula, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701218.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely for a woman named Sempronia Flora, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701219.text_edition: \n Marco Aemilio Fausto Aemiliae Clarae Marco Aemilio Optato Marcus Aemilius \n
EDCS-08701219.translation: To Marcus Aemilius Faustus, to Aemilia Clara, to Marcus Aemilius Optatus, Marcus Aemilius...
EDCS-08701219.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription listing several names, likely family members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701220.text_edition: \n Publi liberta Iucunda annorum LXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XXXXIIII in agro pedes XXVII\n
EDCS-08701220.translation: Iucunda, freedwoman of Publius, aged 70, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. 44 front feet, 27 field feet.
EDCS-08701220.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iucunda, a freedwoman, detailing her age and burial plot dimensions, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701221.text_edition: \n Semproniae Atticae Lucius Aelius Luci filius Galeria Niger Sempronius Atticus testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-08701221.translation: Lucius Aelius Niger, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, and Sempronius Atticus ordered this to be set up in their will for Sempronia Attica.
EDCS-08701221.summary: This funerary inscription records the posthumous dedication of a monument by Lucius Aelius Niger and Sempronius Atticus for Sempronia Attica, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701222.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibia Fir<m=AN>a annorum LV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701222.translation: To the Divine Manes. Vibia Firma, aged 55, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701222.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vibia Firma, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701224.text_edition: \n Proculo Carrucens(i) marito M Proculi uxor ---\n
EDCS-08701224.translation: To Proculus Carrucensius, her husband. M. Proculi's wife...
EDCS-08701224.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to a husband named Proculus Carrucensius by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701226.text_edition: \n Lucius Herennius Maurus hic situs est Bruttia Cai filia Numphe sic umphe hic sita est in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08701226.translation: Lucius Herennius Maurus is buried here. Bruttia, daughter of Caius, Numphe, is buried here. 12 front feet, 10 field feet.
EDCS-08701226.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Herennius Maurus and Bruttia Numphe, specifying plot dimensions, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701227.text_edition: \n Munatius Maurus hic situs est annorum LX sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701227.translation: Munatius Maurus is buried here, aged 60. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701227.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Munatius Maurus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701229.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription containing a name, likely part of a funerary monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701230.text_edition: \n --ara Tuti serva annorum -XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701230.translation: To Tuta, guardian, aged 35 years, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701230.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Tuta, aged 35, with a wish for a light earth.

EDCS-08701231.text_edition: \n Marcus Aninius Adgovilus oriundus ab Aquis ex Gallia hic situs est\n
EDCS-08701231.translation: Marcus Aninius Adgovilus, a native of Aquae in Gaul, is placed here.
EDCS-08701231.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Marcus Aninius Adgovilus, who hailed from Aquae in Gaul.

EDCS-08701232.text_edition: \n Lucio Aninio Marci filio titulum mater kalamitosa sic posuit\n
EDCS-08701232.translation: His mother, the unfortunate Kalamitosa, placed this monument for her son Lucius Aninius, son of Marcus.
EDCS-08701232.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Kalamitosa for her son Lucius Aninius, son of Marcus.

EDCS-08701233.text_edition: \n Decimus Cornelius Decimi filius F M<a>rcellus hic situs est\n
EDCS-08701233.translation: Decimus Cornelius Marcellus, son of Decimus, freedman of Marcus, is placed here.
EDCS-08701233.summary: A funerary epitaph for Decimus Cornelius Marcellus, son of Decimus and freedman of Marcus.

EDCS-08701234.translation: Sius, daughter of V C C A.
EDCS-08701234.summary: An incomplete inscription, likely part of a funerary monument, identifying Sius as the daughter of V C C A.

EDCS-08701236.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph recording the burial of an unnamed individual aged 25.

EDCS-08701237.text_edition: \n RI NI annorum hic sit- est \n
EDCS-08701237.translation: [---] NI aged [---] years is placed here.
EDCS-08701237.summary: An incomplete funerary epitaph recording the burial of an unnamed individual whose name and age are partially preserved.

EDCS-08701238.text_edition: \n In fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XV Domitia Gai liberta Urbana annorum XV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701238.translation: In the front 15 feet, in the field 15 feet. Domitia Urbana, daughter of Gaius, aged 15 years, is placed here; may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701238.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Domitia Urbana, daughter of Gaius, aged 15, and specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-08701240.text_edition: \n Boni Eventus Aponia Montana sacerdos divarum Augustarum coloniae Augustae Firmae editis ob honorem sacerdotii circiensibus et ob dedicationem aliis ex argenti libris CL de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-08701240.translation: Boni Eventus, priestess of the Augustae goddesses of the colony Colonia Augusta Firma, dedicated and presented from her own funds 150 pounds of silver for the honor of the priesthood, for the games, and for the dedication.
EDCS-08701240.summary: An honorary inscription recording the dedication of 150 pounds of silver by the priestess Boni Eventus for religious and public games in the colony of Augusta Firma.

EDCS-08701241.text_edition: \n Aram deo Marti Septiminus rei publicae servus ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-08701241.translation: Septiminus, a servant of the state, erected this altar to the god Mars in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08701241.summary: A votive inscription recording the erection of an altar to Mars by Septiminus, a public servant.

EDCS-08701242.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-08701242.text_edition: \n Publius Numerius Martialis Astigitanus seviralis signum Panthei testamento fieri ponique ex argenti libris C sine ulla deductione iussit\n
EDCS-08701242.translation: Publius Numerius Martialis Astigitanus, a seviral, ordered by his will that a statue of Pantheus be made of 100 pounds of silver, without any deduction, and set up.
EDCS-08701242.summary: This inscription records the posthumous commissioning of a silver statue of Pantheus by Publius Numerius Martialis Astigitanus, a member of the vigintiviri, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701243.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-08701243.text_edition: \n Caecilia Trophime statuam Pietatis ex testamento suo ex argenti pondo C suo et Caecili Silonis mariti sui nomine poni iussit Decimus Caecilius Hospitalis et Caecilia Decimi filia Materna et Caecilia Philete heredes sine ulla deductione XX posuerunt\n
EDCS-08701243.translation: Caecilia Trophime ordered by her will that a statue of Pietas be made of 100 pounds of silver, and its heirs, Decimus Caecilius Hospitalis and Caecilia Materna, daughter of Decimus Caecilius, and Caecilia Philete, set it up in the name of her and her husband, Caecilius Silon, without any deduction of 20 (asses).
EDCS-08701243.summary: This inscription details the posthumous commissioning of a silver statue of Pietas by Caecilia Trophime, with the execution and installation carried out by her heirs, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701244.text_edition: \n Aponia Gai filia Montana nomine Caesi Montani fili sui ex argenti libris C testamento suo poni iussit heredes XX non deduxerunt\n
EDCS-08701244.translation: Aponia Montana, daughter of Gaius, ordered by her will that this be made of 100 pounds of silver in the name of her son, Caesianus Montanus; the heirs did not deduct 20 (asses).
EDCS-08701244.summary: This inscription records the posthumous commissioning of a silver object or monument by Aponia Montana in memory of her son, Caesianus Montanus, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701246.text_edition: \n Cnaeus Manlius Cnaei filius Papiria tribunus cohortis praetoriae praefectus cohortium IIvir praefectus iure dicundo\n
EDCS-08701246.translation: Gnaeus Manlius, son of Gnaeus, of the Papiria tribe, tribune of a praetorian cohort, prefect of cohorts, IIvir, prefect for administering justice.
EDCS-08701246.summary: This inscription lists the career and offices held by Gnaeus Manlius, a Roman official likely of equestrian rank, during the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701248.text_edition: \n E legionis VI victricis piae de sua pecunia fecit\n
EDCS-08701248.translation: Made at his own expense from the legion VI Victrix pia.
EDCS-08701248.summary: This inscription records a dedication made at personal expense by a soldier from the legion VI Victrix, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701250.text_edition: \n Publius Annius - f(ilius) Macer I coloniae Augustae Firmae \n
EDCS-08701250.translation: Publius Annius Macer, son of [name missing], from the colony of Augusta Firma.
EDCS-08701250.summary: This inscription identifies Publius Annius Macer as a citizen of Colonia Augusta Firma, likely dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701251.text_edition: \n Lucius Percius Aper IIvir annorum LX NOV MONV filiae nnorum XX CVM IVRE MA S INE I E pii in suos hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-08701251.translation: Lucius Percius Aper, IIvir, aged 60. His daughter, aged 20, and his wife, aged [number missing] and [name missing], pious in their affections, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701251.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of Lucius Percius Aper, a former IIvir, and his family, with a wish for peaceful rest, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701252.text_edition: \n Caius Cosconius Luci filius Papiria Taurus IIvir et Sulpicia Luci filia Anus hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08701252.translation: Gaius Cosconius Taurus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, IIvir, and Sulpicia Anus, daughter of Lucius. They are buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-08701252.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of Gaius Cosconius Taurus, a former IIvir, and Sulpicia Anus, specifying the dimensions of their plot, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701253.text_edition: \n ius Marci filius Papiria Longinus IIvir bis praefectus ter lacus X cum aeramentis dedit\n
EDCS-08701253.translation: [Name missing], son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, Longinus, IIvir, twice prefect, and X times curator of the aqueduct, dedicated this with bronze fittings.
EDCS-08701253.summary: This inscription details the public career and benefactions of a Roman official named Longinus, who served as IIvir and prefect, and donated bronze fittings for the aqueduct, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701254.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Graecinus coloniae Augustae Firmae servus tabularius nnorum XXXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701254.translation: To the Divine Shades. Graecinus, a clerk from the colony of Augusta Firma, aged 31, pious towards his own people, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701254.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of Graecinus, a clerk from Colonia Augusta Firma, and expresses a pious sentiment towards his family, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701255.text_edition: \n Colonorum coloniae Augustae Firmae ex officina Aureli Cari\n
EDCS-08701255.translation: From the colonists of the colony Augusta Firma, from the workshop of Aurelius Carus.
EDCS-08701255.summary: This inscription records the origin of a group of colonists from Augusta Firma, possibly related to a craft workshop.

EDCS-08701256.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Augustalis sibi et Iuliae\n
EDCS-08701256.translation: Gaius Julius Augustalis for himself and for Julia.
EDCS-08701256.summary: This is a dedication, likely for a tomb or shrine, made by Gaius Julius Augustalis for himself and Julia.

EDCS-08701257.text_edition: \n Decreto decurionum Publio Numerio Martiali Astigitano IIIIIIviro colonorum coloniae Augustae Firmae Publius Numeriustor patrono optimo et indulgentissimo de sua pecunia dedit et editis circiensibus dedicavit\n
EDCS-08701257.translation: By decree of the decurions, Publius Numerius Martialis Astigitanus, IIIIvir of the colonists of the colony Augusta Firma, dedicated to Publius Numerius Martialis, his most excellent and indulgent patron, from his own funds and provided games.
EDCS-08701257.summary: This inscription records a public dedication of games and a monument by Publius Numerius Martialis Astigitanus to his patron, also named Publius Numerius Martialis, in the colony Augusta Firma.

EDCS-08701258.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Hermesiano diffusori oleario patri pientissimo Marcus Iulius Hermes Frontinianus filius et Marcus Iulius Hermesianus nepos posuerunt accepto loco ab splendidissimo ordine Astigitano\n
EDCS-08701258.translation: Marcus Julius Hermes Frontinianus and his grandson Marcus Julius Hermesianus erected [this monument] for Marcus [Julius Hermesianus], their most dutiful father, an oil merchant and distributor, having accepted the site from the most distinguished Astigitan council.
EDCS-08701258.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by sons and grandsons for Marcus Julius Hermesianus, an oil merchant, likely in the city of Astigi.

EDCS-08701259.text_edition: \n Marco nostro Historicus libertus paedagogus de suo dat\n
EDCS-08701259.translation: Our Marcus, Historicus, a freedman and tutor, gives from his own funds.
EDCS-08701259.summary: This inscription records a gift from a freedman named Historicus, who served as a tutor, for someone named Marcus.

EDCS-08701260.text_edition: \n Sabina Valerii Erotis medici uxor annorum L mensium VI pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701260.translation: Sabina, wife of Valerius Erotes, a physician, aged 50 years and 6 months, dutiful, is laid here in her [tomb]; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08701260.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sabina, wife of a physician named Valerius Erotes, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701261.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary marker, mentioning the name Gaius Furnius multiple times.

EDCS-08701262.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Caecilius Secundinus Oni<g=G>itanus nnorum XXIII \n
EDCS-08701262.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Caecilius Secundinus of Onita, aged 23 years.
EDCS-08701262.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Caecilius Secundinus of Onita, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701263.text_edition: \n Lucius Ca<e=C>lius Philocyprius <An=MV>ticariensis pius mulieris libertus hic situs est nnorum XX sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701263.translation: Lucius Caecilius Philocyprius, a freedman of a woman, an 'Anaticarius' (possibly a waterfowl seller or breeder), dutiful, is placed here, aged 20 years; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08701263.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Caecilius Philocyprius, a freedman, possibly a merchant, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701264.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Calpurnius Gaulinianus N<e=A>scaniensis annorum XXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701264.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucius Calpurnius Gaulinianus of Nascanium, aged 35 years, dutiful, is placed here in his [tomb]; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08701264.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Calpurnius Gaulinianus of Nascanium, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701265.text_edition: \n Lucius Lucanius Optatus Carulensis nnum LXXXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701265.translation: Lucius Lucanius Optatus Carulensis, aged 95, pious in his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701265.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Lucanius Optatus Carulensis, who died at the age of 95, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701266.text_edition: \n Publius Numerius Achilles Astigitanus nnorum XCVIII pius in suis hic situs est ILLI VIS CORP MAGNA MERITO ILLI R\n
EDCS-08701266.translation: Publius Numerius Achilles Astigitanus, aged 98, pious in his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon him.
EDCS-08701266.summary: A tombstone for Publius Numerius Achilles Astigitanus, who died at the age of 98, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701267.text_edition: \n Acilia Thiatis nnorum XXV hic sita est dic qui legis sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XXV in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-08701267.translation: Acilia Thiatis, aged 25, lies here. Reader, may the earth lie lightly upon you. 25 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-08701267.summary: A tombstone for Acilia Thiatis, who died at the age of 25, also specifying the dimensions of her burial plot, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701268.text_edition: \n Marcus Aemilius Luci filius Maurus Pudentis nepos hic situs est dicite qui legitis sit vobis sic terra levis in fronte pedes XII in agro X\n
EDCS-08701268.translation: Marcus Aemilius Maurus, son of Lucius, grandson of Pudentis, lies here. You who read, say may the earth lie so lightly upon you. 12 feet in front, 10 in the field.
EDCS-08701268.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Aemilius Maurus, specifying his lineage and the dimensions of his burial plot, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701269.text_edition: \n Aemiliae Marci filiae Quintus Fabius uxori Lucio Fabio Quinti filio \n
EDCS-08701269.translation: To Aemilia, daughter of Marcus. Quintus Fabius [erected this] to his wife, to Lucius Fabius, son of Quintus.
EDCS-08701269.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Aemilia, wife of Quintus Fabius, and their son Lucius Fabius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701270.text_edition: \n Alcume Gai G Firmini serva annorum XXIII\n
EDCS-08701270.translation: To Alcume, slave of Gaius Firminus, aged 23.
EDCS-08701270.summary: A tombstone for Alcume, a slave of Gaius Firminus, who died at the age of 23, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701271.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aninia Patricia nnorum XXXXVII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701271.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aninia Patricia, aged 47, pious in her family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701271.summary: A tombstone for Aninia Patricia, who died at the age of 47, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701272.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius An<t=I>estius <G=C>alba nnorum LXXX pius in suis hic situs est sit <t=I>(ibi) <t=I>(erra) levis \n
EDCS-08701272.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Antestius Alba, aged 80, pious in his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701272.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Antestius Alba, who died at the age of 80, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701273.text_edition: \n Attia Clarina nnorum L hic sita est\n
EDCS-08701273.summary: A tombstone for Attia Clarina, who died at the age of 50, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701274.translation: Sextus Bullius, son of Sextus, [of the tribe] Papiria.
EDCS-08701274.summary: An inscription recording Sextus Bullius, son of Sextus, and mentioning his tribe Papiria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701275.text_edition: \n Caesia Gai filia hic sita est in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701275.translation: Caesia, daughter of Gaius, lies here. 12 feet in front, 10 feet deep. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701275.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caesia, daughter of Gaius, which records the dimensions of her burial plot and includes a common Latin epitaph formula, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701276.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Callistus nnorum III mensium X pius in suis\n
EDCS-08701276.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Callistus, 3 years and 10 months old, dutiful to his own.
EDCS-08701276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to the departed spirits (Dis Manibus) for Callistus, a child who died at the age of 3 years and 10 months, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701277.text_edition: \n Castricia Agathe annorum XVI parentes faciendum curaverunt hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701277.translation: Castricia Agathe, 16 years old. Her parents had this made. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701277.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Castricia Agathe at age 16, stating that her parents commissioned the monument, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701278.text_edition: \n Lucius Coelius Modera<t=N>us Plusia Cai liberta Vegeta uxor hic siti sunt \n
EDCS-08701278.translation: Lucius Coelius Moderatus and Vegeta, freedwoman of Gaius, his wife, lie here.
EDCS-08701278.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Coelius Moderatus and his wife Vegeta, who was a freedwoman, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701280.summary: This is a very brief funerary epitaph stating that Lucius Cornelius lies here, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701281.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cor<neli=DVIT>a Chrestina Cai liberta nnorum XVII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701281.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Cornelia Chrestina, freedwoman of Gaius, 17 years old, dutiful to her own, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701281.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to the departed spirits (Dis Manibus) for Cornelia Chrestina, a 17-year-old freedwoman of Gaius, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701282.text_edition: \n Cornelia Marci filia Paulla annorum XXV hic sita est in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XV sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701282.translation: Cornelia Paula, daughter of Marcus, 25 years old, lies here. 15 feet in front, 15 feet deep. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701282.summary: This funerary epitaph for Cornelia Paula, daughter of Marcus, records her age and the dimensions of her burial plot, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701283.text_edition: \n Cornelia Marci filia Paulla annorum XXV hic sita est in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XV sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701283.translation: Cornelia Paula, daughter of Marcus, 25 years old, lies here. 15 feet in front, 15 feet deep. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701283.summary: This funerary epitaph for Cornelia Paula, daughter of Marcus, records her age and the dimensions of her burial plot, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701285.text_edition: \n Cornelia mulieris liberta Primula annorum XXXIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08701285.translation: Primula, freedwoman of a woman, 33 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. 12 feet in front, 10 feet deep.
EDCS-08701285.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Primula, a freedwoman, at age 33 and specifies the dimensions of her plot, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701286.text_edition: \n Hegloge sic annorum IIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XII\n
EDCS-08701286.translation: Hegloge lies here at the age of 4 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you. 15 feet in front, 12 feet deep.
EDCS-08701286.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Hegloge, a child who died at age 4, and notes the dimensions of the burial plot, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701287.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Eleutheris nnorum XXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701287.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Eleutheris, aged 25, lies here peacefully with her family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701287.summary: A funerary epitaph for Eleutheris, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701288.summary: A very brief funerary epitaph, likely for a woman named Ephapra belonging to the gens Paccia, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701289.text_edition: \n sic nam memor studiis operam praebebat hic situs exanimis cui nomen laudis inhaeret Fabius Fortunatus annorum pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701289.translation: For here, while alive, he devoted himself to studies; here lies lifeless he whose name is synonymous with praise, Fabius Fortunatus, aged [...], dutifully with his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701289.summary: A funerary epitaph for Fabius Fortunatus, a man who was dedicated to his studies, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701290.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Lucius Fabius Saturninus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701291.text_edition: \n Facundus annorum XX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701291.translation: Facundus, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701291.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young man named Facundus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701292.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sextus Fadius Lamyrus annorum L pius in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701292.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sextus Fadius Lamyrus, aged 50, dutifully with his family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701292.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Fadius Lamyrus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701293.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Fannius Venustus nnorum XXXXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701293.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Fannius Venustus, aged 45, dutifully with his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701293.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Fannius Venustus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701294.text_edition: \n Faustina Marci M C liberta annorum XX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701294.translation: Faustina, freedwoman of Marcus Marcius, aged 20, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701294.summary: A funerary epitaph for Faustina, a freedwoman, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701295.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Flavia Firma nnorum XVIII pia in suis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701295.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flavia Firma, aged 18, dutifully with her family. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701295.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young woman, Flavia Firma, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701296.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Flora annorum triginta\n
EDCS-08701296.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Flora, aged thirty.
EDCS-08701296.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Flora, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701297.text_edition: \n Marcus Fulvius Luci filius Rufus hic situs est annorum V\n
EDCS-08701297.translation: Marcus Fulvius Rufus, son of Lucius, aged 5, is buried here.
EDCS-08701297.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Fulvius Rufus, a child who died at age 5.

EDCS-08701298.text_edition: \n Furnia Auctina nnorum XXXX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701298.translation: Furnia Auctina, aged 40, pious in her own way, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701298.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Furnia Auctina, who died at age 40.

EDCS-08701299.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Doc<t=I>ae annorum XXXVI Sentius Agrippa uxori piissimae fecit \n
EDCS-08701299.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sentius Agrippa made this for his most pious wife, Iulia Docta, aged 36.
EDCS-08701299.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Docta, erected by her husband Sentius Agrippa, who died at age 36.

EDCS-08701300.text_edition: \n Iulia Sexta Tiberii Stabilis nnorum XXV hic sita est dic qui legis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701300.translation: Iulia Sexta, aged 25, of Tiberius Stabilis, is buried here. Reader, may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701300.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Sexta, aged 25, possibly related to Tiberius Stabilis.

EDCS-08701301.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuventia Iuniana nnorum plus minus LXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701301.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iuventia Iuniana, aged 75 or more, pious in her own way, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701301.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iuventia Iuniana, who died at approximately age 75.

EDCS-08701302.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus L Euaristus nnorum LX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701302.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus L. Euaristus, aged 60, pious in his own way, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701302.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus L. Euaristus, who died at age 60.

EDCS-08701303.text_edition: \n <L=H>ocus in fronte pedum XII in agro pedum X Marcus Latinius Marsua hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701303.translation: A plot measuring 12 feet in front and 10 feet in the field. Marcus Latinius Marsua is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701303.summary: This inscription records the dimensions of a burial plot and names Marcus Latinius Marsua as the person interred there.

EDCS-08701304.translation: Lucilius, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe.
EDCS-08701304.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely belonging to a tombstone, identifying Lucilius, son of Lucius, and his tribe.

EDCS-08701305.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucretius <T=I>hre<p=R>tus nnorum LXX pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701305.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucretius Threptus, aged 70, pious in his own way, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701305.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucretius Threptus, who died at age 70.

EDCS-08701306.text_edition: \n Marcus Lucretius Sxyustus sic hic situs est sit tibi terra levis te rogo praeteriens ni sic me laedere vellis sic uxoris cura COCI-ITANS--IE \n
EDCS-08701306.translation: Marcus Lucretius Sxyustus lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you. I beg you, as you pass by, unless you wish to hurt me thus, a wife's care...
EDCS-08701306.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Lucretius Sxyustus, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE, lamenting the grief of his wife.

EDCS-08701307.summary: This inscription records the name of Lucretia Paula, daughter of Caius, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-08701308.text_edition: \n Lucretia Cai filia Paula Numeria Firmana filia hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701308.translation: Lucretia Paula, daughter of Caius, and Numeria Firmana, daughter of Paula, are laid here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08701308.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Lucretia Paula and Numeria Firmana, with a common funerary wish.

EDCS-08701310.translation: Gaius Mancinius Maurus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-08701310.summary: This inscription records the name of Gaius Mancinius Maurus, son of Gaius, likely on a tombstone.

EDCS-08701311.text_edition: \n Manilia Moschis nnorum XII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Manilia Tyche nnorum XXII pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Manilius Fuscus nnorum XVIII pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08701311.translation: Manilia Moschis, aged 12, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. Manilia Tyche, aged 22, dutiful to her family, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. Manilius Fuscus, aged 18, dutiful to his family, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. Frontage 12 feet, depth 10 feet.
EDCS-08701311.summary: This tombstone records the burials and ages of Manilia Moschis, Manilia Tyche, and Manilius Fuscus, along with the dimensions of the plot, and includes funerary wishes.

EDCS-08701312.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Manlia Patricia annorum XXXV pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701312.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Manlia Patricia, aged 35, dutiful to her family, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08701312.summary: This tombstone inscription is dedicated to the Divine Spirits and records the burial of Manlia Patricia, noting her age and devotion to her family.

EDCS-08701313.text_edition: \n Lucius Nonius Luci libertus Faustus hic situs est in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08701313.translation: Lucius Nonius Faustus, freedman of Lucius, is laid here. Frontage 12 feet, depth 10 feet.
EDCS-08701313.summary: This inscription records the burial of Lucius Nonius Faustus, a freedman, and specifies the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-08701314.text_edition: \n Lucius Nonius Luci libertus Faustus hic situs est in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08701314.translation: Lucius Nonius Faustus, freedman of Lucius, is laid here. Frontage 12 feet, depth 10 feet.
EDCS-08701314.summary: This inscription records the burial of Lucius Nonius Faustus, a freedman, and specifies the dimensions of his burial plot.

EDCS-08701315.text_edition: \n Lucius Petronius Luci filius Primus hic situs est uxor cara viro monumentum fecit amanti optaram in manibus coniugis occidere quem quia fata nimis rapuerunt tempore iniquo ossibus opto tuis sit pia terra levis\n
EDCS-08701315.translation: Lucius Petronius Primus, son of Lucius, is laid here. His dear wife erected this monument for her loving husband, whom fate snatched away too soon in an unjust time. I wish for your bones, may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08701315.summary: This tombstone commemorates Lucius Petronius Primus, erected by his wife who laments his early death and wishes peace for his remains.

EDCS-08701316.text_edition: \n Fabia Poliniche sic hic sita est in fronte pedes in agro SA NIN\n
EDCS-08701316.translation: Fabia Poliniche is laid here. Frontage in depth SA NIN.
EDCS-08701316.summary: This inscription records the burial of Fabia Poliniche and provides partial measurements for her burial plot.

EDCS-08701317.text_edition: \n Publicia Novata nnum XVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-08701317.translation: Publicia Novata, aged 18, is laid here. May the earth be light upon you. Frontage 12 feet, depth 20 feet.
EDCS-08701317.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Publicia Novata, noting her age and the dimensions of her plot, with a funerary wish.

EDCS-08701318.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Salius Firmanus annorum numero XXII dierum LV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701318.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Salius Firmanus, aged 22 years and 55 days, lies here piously among his own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701318.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Salius Firmanus, recording his age and piety, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701320.text_edition: \n Lucius Servilius Luci libertus Augustinus nnum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis IVL CES AVLXII I CENTVRIoS\n
EDCS-08701320.translation: Lucius Servilius Augustinus, freedman of Lucius, aged 50 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. [Text illegible].
EDCS-08701320.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Servilius Augustinus, a freedman, recording his age and burial location, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701321.text_edition: \n Aulus Sextius Auli filius Papiria Titullus Caerellia Marci liberta Valentina hic siti sunt in fronte pedes X in agro pedes \n
EDCS-08701321.translation: Aulus Sextius Titullus, son of Aulus, of the Papiria tribe, and Valentina, freedwoman of Marcus, of the Caerellia tribe, lie here. In front, 10 feet; in the field [illegible].
EDCS-08701321.summary: This inscription records the burial plot dimensions for Aulus Sextius Titullus and Valentina, a freedwoman, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701322.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Silia Ero nnorum XXXIII? pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701322.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Silia Ero, aged about 33 years, lies here piously among her own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701322.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Silia Ero, recording her age and piety, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701323.text_edition: \n Sulpicia Sexti filia Phoebilla nnorum XXXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701323.translation: Sulpicia Phoebilla, daughter of Sextus, aged 45 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701323.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sulpicia Phoebilla, recording her age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701325.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Lupus annorum LXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701325.translation: Marcus Valerius Lupus, aged 62 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701325.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Lupus, recording his age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701326.text_edition: \n Valeria Luci filia na nnorum LV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Marcus Marcius Marci filius Papiria Marcellus nnorum LXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701326.translation: Valeria, daughter of Lucius, aged 55 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcus Marcius Marcellus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe, aged 70 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701326.summary: This inscription is a double funerary epitaph for Valeria and Marcus Marcius Marcellus, recording their ages and tribal affiliations, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701327.text_edition: \n Valeriae Marci filiae Tertullae annorum XIIX Servilia Luci filia mater de sua pecunia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08701327.translation: Servilia, daughter of Lucius, her mother, had this made from her own money for Valeria Tertulla, daughter of Marcus, aged 18 years.
EDCS-08701327.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording that Servilia commissioned the monument for her daughter Valeria Tertulla, indicating the daughter's age, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701328.text_edition: \n Valia Marci liberta Paula nnorum XXXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Marcus Valius Priscus Marci Baebi libertus nnorum XXII Marcus Valius Bassus Marci libertus nnorum XX Marcus Valius Romanus Marci libertus nnorum XXXII Baebia Marci filia Crispina nnorum XX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701328.translation: Valia Paula, freedwoman of Marcus, aged 45 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Marcus Valius Priscus, freedman of Marcus, aged 22 years. Marcus Valius Bassus, freedman of Marcus, aged 20 years. Marcus Valius Romanus, freedman of Marcus, aged 32 years. Baebia Crispina, daughter of Marcus, aged 20 years, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701328.summary: This inscription is a complex funerary epitaph recording the burial of Valia Paula and multiple freedmen and a daughter of Marcus Valius, along with their ages, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701329.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Venaecius Sollers nnum LXV pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701329.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Venaecius Sollers, aged 65 years, lies here piously among his own. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Venaecius Sollers, recording his age and piety, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701330.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vesonia Felicitas nnorum LVIII pia in suis\n
EDCS-08701330.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Vesonia Felicitas, aged 58, dutiful to her family.
EDCS-08701330.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vesonia Felicitas, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08701331.text_edition: \n Marco Vibio Marci filio Papiria Campano Caius Marcius Linus et Marcia Pusinna\n
EDCS-08701331.translation: To Marcus Vibius Campanus, son of Marcus, of the Papiria tribe. Caius Marcius Linus and Marcia Pusinna [set this up].
EDCS-08701331.summary: A funerary dedication, likely for Marcus Vibius Campanus, set up by Caius Marcius Linus and Marcia Pusinna, probably from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-08701332.text_edition: \n Lucius Vibius Luci filius Papiria Rusticus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XIIII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08701332.translation: Lucius Vibius Rusticus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. 14 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-08701332.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial plot dimensions for Lucius Vibius Rusticus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08701333.text_edition: \n Volusia Prima hic sita est sit tibi terra levis in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-08701333.translation: Volusia Prima lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. 12 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-08701333.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial plot dimensions for Volusia Prima, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08701334.summary: A fragmented inscription likely recording a patronymic or familial relationship, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701336.text_edition: \n CLannorum XVII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701336.translation: Aged 17, dutiful to his family, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701336.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young man aged 17, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701345.summary: A fragmented inscription likely referring to a family or a group, possibly a dedication or a property marker.

EDCS-08701346.translation: Of Lucius Vettius, son of Caius.
EDCS-08701346.summary: A fragmented inscription likely indicating ownership or a dedication, referring to Lucius Vettius, son of Caius, probably from the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-08701357.text_edition: \n <T=P>iche sic Quinti Cutani serva nnorum XXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Decimus Avius Paetanius posuit \n
EDCS-08701357.translation: Tiche, servant of Quintus Cutanius, aged 22, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Decimus Avius Paetanius set [this up].
EDCS-08701357.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiche, a servant girl, set up by Decimus Avius Paetanius, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08701359.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate X imperator XX consul IX pater patriae censor viam Augustam ab Iano ad Oceanum refecit poantes fecit veteres restituit\n
EDCS-08701359.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 10th time, imperator for the 20th time, consul for the 9th time, father of the fatherland, censor, rebuilt the Via Augusta from Janus to the Ocean, constructed bridges, restored the old ones.
EDCS-08701359.summary: An official inscription from Emperor Vespasian (reigned 69-79 CE) detailing the repair and construction of the Via Augusta.

EDCS-08701361.text_edition: \n Caciae nostrae Lucius Sempronius Suavis dat\n
EDCS-08701361.translation: Lucius Sempronius Suavis dedicates this to our Cacia.
EDCS-08701361.summary: A dedication by Lucius Sempronius Suavis to his Cacia, probably a tombstone from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701362.translation: Belonging to Numerius Cordius, son of Numerius; [and?] Numerius Terentius.
EDCS-08701362.summary: A fragmented inscription listing names, possibly indicating ownership or a tomb, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701363.text_edition: \n ucius Quinti filius Papiria Augusta Firma decurio? alae Parthorum |(centurio) legionum VI et IV IIvir mia Publi filia Polla mater ucius Quinti filius frater\n
EDCS-08701363.translation: Lucius, son of Quintus, of the Papiria tribe, Augusta Firma, decurion? of the ala Parthorum, centurion of legions VI and IV, duovir, mia, daughter of Publius, Polla, mother, Lucius, son of Quintus, brother.
EDCS-08701363.summary: A complex funerary inscription detailing the military and civic career of Lucius Quinti, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701364.text_edition: \n Lucius Virrius Luci filius Papiria Fidus nepos militaris legionis VI hic situs est ex testamento locus in fronte pedum XVII in agro XVI\n
EDCS-08701364.translation: Lucius Virrius Fidus, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, grandson of Lucius, a soldier of the VI legion, lies here by his will; the plot is 17 feet in front and 16 feet deep.
EDCS-08701364.summary: A funerary epitaph specifying burial arrangements and plot dimensions for Lucius Virrius Fidus, a soldier of the VI Legion, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701365.text_edition: \n Lucio Virrio Luci filio Papiria Senecioni ex testamento fili locus in fronte pedum XVI in agro pedum XVI\n
EDCS-08701365.translation: To Lucius Virrius Senecio, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, by his son's will; the plot is 16 feet in front and 16 feet deep.
EDCS-08701365.summary: A funerary inscription detailing burial arrangements and plot dimensions for Lucius Virrius Senecio, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701366.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cocceia Cinna<m=N>is nnum CVI pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-08701366.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Cocceia Cinna, aged 106, dutiful to her family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701366.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cocceia Cinna, recording her age and piety, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701367.text_edition: \n Titucia Quinti liberta Felicula annorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701367.translation: Titucia Felicula, freedwoman of Quintus, aged 50, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701367.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titucia Felicula, a freedwoman, recording her age, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701369.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Avia Quinti Villibia nnorum LXXXXIII pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701369.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Avia Villibia, aged 93, dutiful to her family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701369.summary: A funerary epitaph for Avia Villibia, recording her age and piety, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701370.text_edition: \n mia Luci filia Drachume annorum pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701370.translation: mia, daughter of Lucius, aged [...], dutiful to her family, lies here; may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701370.summary: A funerary epitaph for mia, daughter of Lucius, recording her piety, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701371.text_edition: \n Petronia Cai liberta Lyde in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-08701371.translation: Lyde, freedwoman of Petronia Cai, 12 feet in front, 10 feet in the field.
EDCS-08701371.summary: This inscription records land measurements related to a property belonging to Petronia Cai's freedwoman, Lyde.

EDCS-08701372.text_edition: \n Decreto decurionum Clodiae Sabinae Caius Clodius Turrin libertus Epaphroditus \n
EDCS-08701372.translation: By decree of the town council, Caius Clodius Turrin, freedman of Epaphroditus, for the Clodiae Sabinae.
EDCS-08701372.summary: This inscription records a decree by the town council of Clodiae Sabinae honoring Caius Clodius Turrin, a freedman of Epaphroditus, likely to erect a monument for him.

EDCS-08701373.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Servilia Psyche annorum XX pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701373.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Servilia Psyche, aged 20, pious towards her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701373.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Servilia Psyche, aged 20, recorded by her family, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701376.text_edition: \n Tito Mercelloni Persino Mario procuratori Augusti decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-08701376.translation: To Titus Marcellinus Persinus, procurator of Augustus, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-08701376.summary: This inscription honors Titus Marcellinus Persinus, a procurator of Augustus, by decree of the town council, likely for a public monument.

EDCS-08701377.text_edition: \n Maxumus sic huic ordo Augurinorum Segidensium statuam locum sepulturae funeris impensam decrevit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701377.translation: Maxumus. For him, the order of the Augurines of Segusium decreed a statue, a burial place, and funeral expenses. He is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701377.summary: This funerary inscription records the posthumous honors granted by the college of Augurines of Segusium to Maxumus, including a statue, burial plot, and funeral expenses, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701381.translation: For Aulus Cilo. / For Aulus Cilo.
EDCS-08701381.summary: This inscription is fragmentary and likely marks the property or tomb of Aulus Cilo, possibly dating to the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-08701386.text_edition: \n Caecilia Quinti liberta Felicula pia in suis hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701386.translation: Caecilia Felicula, freedwoman of Quintus, pious towards her own, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701386.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia Felicula, freedwoman of Quintus, recorded by her family, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701387.text_edition: \n Lucius Fulvius Marci libertus Philomusus hic situs est est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701387.translation: Lucius Fulvius Philomusus, freedman of Marcus, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08701387.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Fulvius Philomusus, freedman of Marcus, recorded by his family, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701390.summary: This inscription likely indicates ownership or a dedication by Aemilius Priscus, a freedman, possibly on a tomb or other structure.

EDCS-08701393.text_edition: \n Bucolus annis XXX fuit ia Romana \n
EDCS-08701393.translation: Bucolus was 30 years old. Roman citizen.
EDCS-08701393.summary: This is a brief funerary inscription for Bucolus, who was 30 years old and a Roman citizen, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08701394.summary: This inscription is very fragmentary, possibly recording a name (Corinthus) and numerical data, its exact purpose being unclear but possibly related to accounts or measures.

EDCS-08701395.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publius Aemilius Secundus Patriciensis annorum VL pius in suos hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701395.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Aemilius Secundus, a Patrician, aged 60, pious towards his family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701395.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Aemilius Secundus, a pious Patrician, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-08701396.text_edition: \n Maurus Volusii Umbri filius annorum LXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701396.translation: Maurus, son of Volusius Umber, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701396.summary: This is a tombstone for Maurus, son of Volusius Umber, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-08701398.summary: This inscription likely indicates that sons erected this monument or memorial.

EDCS-08701399.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Successa nnorum XXV pia in suos hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701399.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Successa, aged 25, pious towards her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701399.summary: This is a tombstone for Successa, who died at the age of 25 and was known for her piety towards her family.

EDCS-08701400.text_edition: \n Aemiliae Cai filiae Rusticae post mortem Lucretia Lucilla mater posuit\n
EDCS-08701400.translation: Lucretia Lucilla, mother, placed [this] for Aemilia Rustica, daughter of Cai, after death.
EDCS-08701400.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucretia Lucilla for her deceased daughter, Aemilia Rustica.

EDCS-08701404.text_edition: \n Obulculensis nnorum CII hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis Rustica Caccossae filia nnorum LXXII hic sita est dicite qui legitis sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701404.translation: Of Obulculensis, aged 102, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Rustica, daughter of Caccossa, aged 72, is buried here. Tell this to those who read: may the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701404.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of two individuals, Obulculensis (aged 102) and Rustica (aged 72).

EDCS-08701407.text_edition: \n Dorus Lucretiae Afrae servus nnum XIIX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701407.translation: Dorus, slave of Lucretia Afra, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701407.summary: This is a tombstone for Dorus, a slave of Lucretia Afra, who died at the age of 18.

EDCS-08701408.summary: This inscription likely refers to land boundaries or measurements associated with a person named Sabina.

EDCS-08701409.text_edition: \n Cai Manli Cnaei fili Sergia Toloconis <in=LA> <f=T>ronte XLXI \n
EDCS-08701409.translation: Cai Manlius, son of Cnaeus, and Sergia Toloconis, 40 [or 60?] on the front...
EDCS-08701409.summary: This inscription appears to record names and possibly property measurements or details related to Cai Manlius and Sergia Toloconis.

EDCS-08701410.summary: A dedication to the sun god Sol Invictus Augustus, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-08701414.text_edition: \n Fabiae Rusticae matri annorum LXXXII Lucio Fabio Mauro filio annorum XXVIII Cornelia Optata faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-08701414.translation: To Fabia Rustica, mother, aged 82. Lucius Fabio Mauro, son, aged 28, had this made. Cornelia Optata saw to its construction.
EDCS-08701414.summary: A funerary monument erected by Cornelia Optata for her mother Fabia Rustica and son Lucius Fabio Mauro, likely dated to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08701416.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription possibly naming Gaius Sempronius, son of Lucius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701418.text_edition: \n Amanda Rustici serva nnorum XIIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-08701418.translation: Amanda, Rusticus's slave woman, aged 14, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08701418.summary: A tombstone for Amanda, a slave girl aged 14, erected by Rusticus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-08701423.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Flavius Flavianus nnorum XXXXVIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Dis Manibus sacrum Romulensia nnorum XXXXV mensium VI hic sita est sit terra levis\n
EDCS-08701423.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Flavius Flavianus, aged 48, pious among his own, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sacred to the departed spirits. Romulensia, aged 45 years and 6 months, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly.
EDCS-08701423.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Lucius Flavius Flavianus and Romulensia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-08901006.text_edition: \n Iunoni Augustae sacrum in honorem Alfiae Domitiae Severianae flaminicae perpetuae ex decreto splendidissimi ordinis cui statuam argenteam ex argenti pondo C poni decreverat Caius Iulius Alfius Theseus et Alfia Domitia Tertullina parentes et Quintus Alfius Iulius Severus Optatianus frater ex argenti pondo C faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-08901006.translation: Sacred to Juno Augusta, in honor of Alfia Domitia Severiana, perpetual priestess, by decree of the most splendid order, which had decided that a silver statue weighing 100 pounds should be erected. Gaius Julius Alfius Theseus and Alfia Domitia Tertullina, parents, and Quintus Alfius Julius Severus Optatianus, brother, had this made from 100 pounds of silver.
EDCS-08901006.summary: A dedication of a silver statue to Juno Augusta in honor of Alfia Domitia Severiana, funded by her family, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-09000001.text_edition: \n SANTIMS Apollini votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-09000001.translation: To the holy Apollo, he willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-09000001.summary: A votive offering to Apollo, recording the fulfillment of a vow by an unnamed individual, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000003.text_edition: \n Marcus Publicius populi libertus Victor votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09000003.translation: Marcus Publicius Victor, freedman of the people, willingly fulfilled his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-09000003.summary: A votive inscription by Marcus Publicius Victor, a freedman, recording the fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000004.text_edition: \n Augusto Paci perpetuae et Concordiae Augustae Quintus Vibius Felicio sevir et Vibia Felicula ministra Tutelae Augustae de sua pecunia dederunt dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-09000004.translation: To Augustus Pacis Perpetuae and Concordia Augusta, Quintus Vibius Felicio, sevir, and Vibia Felicula, minister of Tutela Augusta, dedicated and consecrated from their own money.
EDCS-09000004.summary: A dedication of offerings to personifications of peace and concord by a sevir and a minister, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.
