EDCS-09000961.translation: Publius Pomponius Sulpicianus, son of Publius, of the Papirian tribe, aged 17, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Sulpicia Callirhoe, freedwoman, mother, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000961.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Publius Pomponius Sulpicianus, aged 17, and his mother Sulpicia Callirhoe, aged 50, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000962.text_edition: \n Acidus nnorum XXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis occisus\n
EDCS-09000962.translation: Acidus, aged 22, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Killed.
EDCS-09000962.summary: This tombstone records the death of Acidus, aged 22, who was killed, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000963.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Publi filius Pupillus Lucretia Marci filia uxsor sic\n
EDCS-09000963.translation: Publius Cornelius Pupillus, son of Publius, and Lucretia, daughter of Marcus, wife, [lie here].
EDCS-09000963.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone recording the burial of Publius Cornelius Pupillus and his wife Lucretia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000964.text_edition: \n Corneliae Publi filiae Annocae Marcus Cornelius Priscinus amitae\n
EDCS-09000964.translation: Marcus Cornelius Priscinus to his aunt Annoca, daughter of Publius.
EDCS-09000964.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Marcus Cornelius Priscinus to his aunt Annoca, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000965.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a tombstone, recording the name of Lucius Herennius, a freedman.

EDCS-09000966.text_edition: \n Herennia Cai liberta Fausta hic sit- sunt sit vobis terra levis Quintus Vinnius Quinti libertus Placidus annorum XLV\n
EDCS-09000966.translation: Herennia Fausta, freedwoman of Caius, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Quintus Vinnius Placidus, freedman of Quintus, aged 45 [lies here].
EDCS-09000966.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Herennia Fausta and Quintus Vinnius Placidus, aged 45, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000967.text_edition: \n Licinus Gaviae Faustae servus annorum XV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000967.translation: Licinus, slave of Gavia Faustus, aged 15, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000967.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Licinus, a slave of Gavia Faustus, at the age of 15, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000968.text_edition: \n Publius Manlius Publi filius Manlianus nnorum XVIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000968.translation: Publius Manlius Manlianus, son of Publius, aged 18, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000968.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Publius Manlius Manlianus at the age of 18, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000969.text_edition: \n Maura Quinti Valeri serva nnorum XXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Quintus Valerius Mode nnorum LXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Nigrina Setia nnorum L hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000969.translation: Maura, slave of Quintus Valerius, aged 22, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Quintus Valerius Mode, aged 75, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Nigrina Setia, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000969.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Maura, a slave, Quintus Valerius Mode, and Nigrina Setia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09000970.text_edition: \n Octavia Titi filia Secunda annorum XXXII mater optima hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000970.translation: Octavia Secunda, daughter of Titus, aged 32, the best mother, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000970.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Octavia Secunda, a devoted mother, in her early thirties.

EDCS-09000971.text_edition: \n Parilia Silvana filia nnorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000971.translation: Parilia, daughter of Silvanus, aged 25, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000971.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Parilia, daughter of Silvanus, at the age of 25.

EDCS-09000972.text_edition: \n hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis Vernio <s=C>(ua) pecunia dedit \n
EDCS-09000972.translation: Here lies. May the earth lie lightly on you. Vernio dedicated (this) with his own money.
EDCS-09000972.summary: A tombstone erected by Vernio for himself or a relative, funded by his personal money.

EDCS-09000973.text_edition: \n Publius Vicius Rufus annorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Manilia Laeta annorum LXX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000973.translation: Publius Vicius Rufus, aged 50, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Manilia Laeta, aged 70, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000973.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of both Publius Vicius Rufus and Manilia Laeta.

EDCS-09000975.translation: L. IDAV placed (this) at his own expense.
EDCS-09000975.summary: A funerary monument indicating that L. IDAV paid for its construction from his own funds.

EDCS-09000976.text_edition: \n Tongilia Titi filia Maxuma Scaevini Emeritensis annorum LX sibi et Lucio Granio Luci filio Papiria Scaevino viro nnorum LXXXV de sua pecunia faciendum curavit hic siti sunt sit vobis terra levis\n
EDCS-09000976.translation: Tongilia Maxuma, daughter of Titus, from Scaevinus of Emerita, aged 60, arranged for this to be made at her own expense for herself and for her husband Lucius Granius, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, of Scaevinus, aged 85. They lie here. May the earth lie lightly on you both.
EDCS-09000976.summary: A tombstone commissioned by Tongilia Maxuma for herself and her husband Lucius Granius, detailing their ages and tribal affiliations.

EDCS-09000977.translation: Quintus Cornelius Quartus (dedicates this) to Jupiter.
EDCS-09000977.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Quintus Cornelius Quartus.

EDCS-09000978.text_edition: \n Quintus Aelius Rusticus annorum LXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000978.translation: Quintus Aelius Rusticus, aged 65, lies here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09000978.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Quintus Aelius Rusticus at the age of 65.

EDCS-09000980.text_edition: \n hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis annorum XXI DAINV Fabia Sera annorum \n
EDCS-09000980.translation: Here lies. May the earth lie lightly on you. Aged 21. DAINV Fabia Sera, aged...
EDCS-09000980.summary: A partially preserved tombstone for Fabia Sera, indicating her age at death as 21, with a lacuna for her surname.

EDCS-09000981.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lutetia of the family Saeliensis.
EDCS-09000981.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Manes by Lutetia of the Saeliensis family.

EDCS-09000982.text_edition: \n Servolus Gai Deci Italici servus nnorum LXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09000982.translation: Servolus, the slave of Gaius Decius Italicus, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09000982.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Servolus, aged 80, slave of Gaius Decius Italicus.

EDCS-09000983.text_edition: \n Cantia Cai liberta Clarina annorum XXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Restitutus de suo faciendum quravit\n
EDCS-09000983.translation: Clarina, freedwoman of Caius, aged 25, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Restitutus took care of the construction at his own expense.
EDCS-09000983.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Clarina, freedwoman of Caius, aged 25, with construction paid for by Restitutus.

EDCS-09000984.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Publi libertus Quintio annorum LV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis ex testamento Lucius Granius Severus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09000984.translation: Publius Cornelius Quintio, freedman of Publius, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Granius Severus took care of the construction according to the will.
EDCS-09000984.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Publius Cornelius Quintio, freedman of Publius, aged 55, with construction arranged by Lucius Granius Severus as stipulated in a will.

EDCS-09000985.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Publi libertus Quintio annorum LV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis ex testamento Lucius Granius Severus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09000985.translation: Publius Cornelius Quintio, freedman of Publius, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Lucius Granius Severus took care of the construction according to the will.
EDCS-09000985.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Publius Cornelius Quintio, freedman of Publius, aged 55, with construction arranged by Lucius Granius Severus as stipulated in a will.

EDCS-09000986.text_edition: \n Cornelia Quinti filia Marcella pia hic sita est Iucundus servus de suo\n
EDCS-09000986.translation: Cornelia Marcella, pious daughter of Quintus, is buried here. Iucundus, a slave, [paid for this] at his own expense.
EDCS-09000986.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Cornelia Marcella, daughter of Quintus, with the construction paid for by a slave named Iucundus.

EDCS-09000987.text_edition: \n na annorum XX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Eromene mater de suo fecit\n
EDCS-09000987.translation: aged 20, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Eromene, the mother, made [this] at her own expense.
EDCS-09000987.summary: A tombstone records the burial of an unnamed 20-year-old, with the monument erected by her mother, Eromene, at her own expense.

EDCS-09000988.text_edition: \n Caius Flavius Cai libertus Philocalus hic situs est Anis heres j de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09000988.translation: Caius Flavius Philocalus, freedman of Caius, is buried here. Anis, the heir, took care of the construction at his own expense.
EDCS-09000988.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Caius Flavius Philocalus, freedman of Caius, with construction arranged by his heir, Anis.

EDCS-09000989.text_edition: \n Norbana Lucilla Luci Norbani Vettonis filia annorum XV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Sempronius Libiensis de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09000989.translation: Norbana Lucilla, daughter of Lucius Norbanus Vettonis, aged 15, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Sempronius Libiensis took care of the construction at his own expense.
EDCS-09000989.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Norbana Lucilla, aged 15, daughter of Lucius Norbanus Vettonis, with construction paid for by Sempronius Libiensis.

EDCS-09000990.text_edition: \n Silvana ani serva annorum V hic sita est S AGATO heres de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09000990.translation: Silvana, slave of Ani, aged 5, is buried here. S. Agatho, heir, took care of the construction at his own expense.
EDCS-09000990.summary: A tombstone records the burial of Silvana, slave of Ani, aged 5, with construction arranged by her heir, S. Agatho.

EDCS-09000991.text_edition: \n Herculi Lucius Cornelius Firmillus et Lucius Porcius Lucanus Augustales dedicaverunt\n
EDCS-09000991.translation: Lucius Cornelius Firmillus and Lucius Porcius Lucanus, Augustales, dedicated [this] to Hercules.
EDCS-09000991.summary: An altar or dedication records that Lucius Cornelius Firmillus and Lucius Porcius Lucanus, members of the Augustales, dedicated a monument to Hercules.

EDCS-09000992.text_edition: \n Laribus Augustis aram Lucius Cornelius Firmillus Augustalis de suo dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09000992.translation: Lucius Cornelius Firmillus, an Augustalis, dedicated and consecrated this altar to the household gods of Augustus at his own expense.
EDCS-09000992.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to the Lares Augusti by Lucius Cornelius Firmillus.

EDCS-09000993.text_edition: \n Caius Cavius Cai filius Papiria Scaevaus hic situs nepos sua ITA impensa II\n
EDCS-09000993.translation: Gaius Cavius Scaevus, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, is buried here, his nephew funded the tomb at his own expense.
EDCS-09000993.summary: This inscription records the burial of Gaius Cavius Scaevus and the funding of his tomb by his nephew.

EDCS-09000994.text_edition: \n Marcus Loreius Martialis annorum LX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Aemilia Munica de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09000994.translation: Marcus Loreius Martialis, aged 60, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Aemilia Munica arranged for this to be made at her own expense.
EDCS-09000994.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Loreius Martialis and the provision of the monument by Aemilia Munica.

EDCS-09000995.text_edition: \n Petronia Luci filia Prisca annorum XXXV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis L Davus posuit\n
EDCS-09000995.translation: Petronia Prisca, daughter of Lucius, aged 35, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. L. Davus set this up.
EDCS-09000995.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Petronia Prisca and the monument erected by L. Davus.

EDCS-09000996.translation: Tonceta, daughter of Quintus, of the Silvan family, is buried here.
EDCS-09000996.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Tonceta.

EDCS-09000999.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Bonosa Cai filia Procula nnum XXXVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Fabia Bonosa mater de sua pecunia faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09000999.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Bonosa Procula, daughter of Gaius, aged 38, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Her mother, Fabia Bonosa, arranged for this to be made at her own expense.
EDCS-09000999.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Bonosa Procula and the monument provided by her mother.

EDCS-09001000.text_edition: \n Lucius Marcius Rusticus Victor annorum LXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Tabla faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09001000.translation: Lucius Marcius Rusticus Victor, aged 70, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Tabla arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-09001000.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Marcius Rusticus Victor and the monument arranged by Tabla.

EDCS-09001001.text_edition: \n Marcus Cornelius Proculus aedilis de sua pecunia dat\n
EDCS-09001001.translation: Marcus Cornelius Proculus, the aedile, gives this at his own expense.
EDCS-09001001.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Marcus Cornelius Proculus in his capacity as aedile.

EDCS-09001003.text_edition: \n Marcus Aemilius Reburrus Turi annorum LIX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001003.translation: Marcus Aemilius Reburrus Turi, aged 59, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001003.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Marcus Aemilius Reburrus Turi.

EDCS-09001004.text_edition: \n Caius Rutilius Urbanus Turi annorum LXXX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001004.translation: Gaius Rutilius Urbanus Turi, aged 80, is buried here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001004.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Gaius Rutilius Urbanus Turi.

EDCS-09001006.text_edition: \n Sextus Iuius filius Ianuarius Papiria annorum XXXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis faciendum curavit filius\n
EDCS-09001006.translation: Sextus Julius Ianuarius, son of Julius, aged 45, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. His son had this made.
EDCS-09001006.summary: A funerary epitaph for Sextus Julius Ianuarius, commissioned by his son.

EDCS-09001007.translation: Boutia, daughter of Cadarus, is buried here.
EDCS-09001007.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Boutia.

EDCS-09001008.text_edition: \n Caius Sulpicius Taurus Emeritensis annorum XVI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Felix ancilla de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09001008.translation: Gaius Sulpicius Taurus from Emerita, aged 16, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. Felix, a slave woman, had this made at her own expense.
EDCS-09001008.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Sulpicius Taurus, commissioned by a slave woman named Felix.

EDCS-09001009.summary: The beginning of a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph or dedication.

EDCS-09001010.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly the name of an individual on a monument.

EDCS-09001012.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Luci filio Galeria Silvano Vict Valeriano Vict \n
EDCS-09001012.translation: Lucius Valerius Silvanus, son of Lucius, from the Galeria tribe, and Vict[orius] Valerianus Vict[orius]...
EDCS-09001012.summary: A fragmentary inscription listing individuals, possibly related to a family monument or legal document.

EDCS-09001013.text_edition: \n Lutatia Avita annorum XVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001013.translation: Lutatia Avita, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09001013.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lutatia Avita.

EDCS-09001014.text_edition: \n Lutatia Avita nnorum XVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001014.translation: Lutatia Avita, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09001014.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lutatia Avita, likely a duplicate or variation of EDCS-09001013.

EDCS-09001015.text_edition: \n Lutatia Avita annorum XVIII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001015.translation: Lutatia Avita, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-09001015.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lutatia Avita, likely a duplicate or variation of EDCS-09001013 and EDCS-09001014.

EDCS-09001016.text_edition: \n annorum LXX hic sit- est sit tibi terra levis curaverunt faciendum L IVSINA et NICELA\n
EDCS-09001016.translation: ...aged 70, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you. L. Iusina and Nicela had this made.
EDCS-09001016.summary: A funerary epitaph for an unnamed individual aged 70, commissioned by L. Iusina and Nicela.

EDCS-09001017.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesare Augusto Germanico imperatore \n
EDCS-09001017.translation: Under the emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus.
EDCS-09001017.summary: This is an inscription commemorating Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, likely from the period of his reign (41-54 AD).

EDCS-09001018.text_edition: \n inius Gai filius Galeria Lupus mil<e=I>s <l=I>egionis IIII Macedonicae e<q=O>ues hic situs est sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-09001018.translation: Lupus, son of Gaius, of the Galerian tribe, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001018.summary: This is a tombstone for Lupus, a soldier of the Fourth Macedonian Legion, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001019.text_edition: \n cilius (3) filius Ianus iterum Caius Sulpicius Cai filius Lucius Aemilius (3) filius Publius Valerius \n
EDCS-09001019.translation: Cilius, son of Ianus, again Gaius Sulpicius, son of Caius, Lucius Aemilius, son of [?], Publius Valerius.
EDCS-09001019.summary: This inscription lists several Roman names, possibly indicating a group of individuals or a dedication by multiple people, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001020.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cantia Celtibera Emeritensis annorum XXXV Lucius V I uxori suae posuit de suo hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001020.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Cantia Celtibera, from Emerita, aged 35, Lucius V I placed this for his wife. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius V I for his wife Cantia Celtibera, who died at age 35, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001021.text_edition: \n Nic<e=F>ros Arsensis annorum LVIII hic situs sit tibi terra levis \n
EDCS-09001021.translation: Nicerus of Arsa, aged 58, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001021.summary: This is a tombstone for Nicerus of Arsa, who died at age 58, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001022.text_edition: \n Publius Fabius Modestus Rufionis filius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Publius Acilius Rufus sobrino de suo posuit\n
EDCS-09001022.translation: Publius Fabius Modestus, son of Rufio, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Publius Acilius Rufus placed this for his nephew at his own expense.
EDCS-09001022.summary: This is a tombstone for Publius Fabius Modestus, for whom his nephew Publius Acilius Rufus erected a monument, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001023.translation: Musa, a slave of Sabinus, aged 10.
EDCS-09001023.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Musa, a slave of Sabinus, at the age of 10, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001026.text_edition: \n Lucius Trebicius Luci filius Galeria Faustinus annorum L hic situs est sit tibi terra levis T F F de su- P\n
EDCS-09001026.translation: Lucius Trebicius Faustinus, son of Lucius, of the Galerian tribe, aged 50, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. T. F. F. from his own [property].
EDCS-09001026.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Trebicius Faustinus, who died at age 50, erected by a relative, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001028.text_edition: \n Genio oppidi Iustus Modesti filius Xvir maximus ponendam curavit\n
EDCS-09001028.translation: Iustus, son of Modestus, the chief magistrate (Xvir maximus), saw to its erection for the Genius of the town.
EDCS-09001028.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to the Genius of the town, arranged by Iustus Modesti, a local official, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001029.text_edition: \n Iunoni sacrum Terentia Puella testamento poni iussit ex argenti libris L\n
EDCS-09001029.translation: Sacred to Juno. Terentia Puella ordered this to be set up in her will, from 50 pounds of silver.
EDCS-09001029.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Juno, funded from 50 pounds of silver as stipulated in the will of Terentia Puella, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001030.text_edition: \n Templum Pietatis Augustae vetustate conlapsum res publica Reginensium sumptu suo refecit curantibus Quinto F Herenniano et Caio F Taurino\n
EDCS-09001030.translation: The Republic of the Reginensians repaired at its own expense the Temple of the Augustan Piety, which had collapsed from age, under the supervision of Quintus F. Herennianus and Gaius F. Taurinus.
EDCS-09001030.summary: This inscription records the repair of the Temple of Piety by the local government of Reginensium, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-09001032.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Caesari Augusto Germanico imperatori \n
EDCS-09001032.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, the Emperor.
EDCS-09001032.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Tiberius Claudius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, found in Reginensium and likely dating to his reign (41-54 CE).

EDCS-09001034.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Severo Pio Augusto Felici Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti filio Arabico Adiabenico Parthico maximo Britannico maximo patri patriae res publica Reginensium devota numini eius posuit\n
EDCS-09001034.translation: The Republic of the Reginensians, devoted to his divine spirit, dedicated this to Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Severus Pius Augustus Felix, son of Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, Great Arabicus, Great Adiabenicus, Great Parthicus, Great Britannicus, father of the fatherland.
EDCS-09001034.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the Republic of Reginensium to Emperor Caracalla, dating to his reign (198-217 CE).

EDCS-09001035.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Privatus dispensator et verna Augusti hic iacet cum suo alumno Praesenti de suo peculio\n
EDCS-09001035.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Privatus, steward and household slave of Augustus, lies here with his adopted son Praesentis, from his own peculium.
EDCS-09001035.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Privatus, a steward and slave, and his adopted son Praesentis, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09001037.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Calpurnius Viator, likely a dedication or a tombstone fragment from the Roman period.

EDCS-09001038.text_edition: \n Corneliae Severianae ex testamento Publi Numisi Superstitis Cocceia Severa dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09001038.translation: Cocceia Severa gave and dedicated this to Cornelia Severiana, according to her will.
EDCS-09001038.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Cocceia Severa to Cornelia Severiana, made according to a will, probably in the Imperial period.

EDCS-09001039.text_edition: \n Publio Numisio Superstiti ex testamento suo Cocceia Severa dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-09001039.translation: Cocceia Severa gave and dedicated this to Publius Numisius Superstitis, according to his will.
EDCS-09001039.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Cocceia Severa to Publius Numisius Superstitis, made according to his will, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-09001041.text_edition: \n biae Chrysote Reginensi uxori C COA Reginensis fecit\n
EDCS-09001041.translation: C. Coa Reginensis made this for his wife, Biae Chrysote Reginensis.
EDCS-09001041.summary: This inscription is a dedication made by C. Coa Reginensis to his wife, Biae Chrysote Reginensis, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-09001042.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Rufinius Primus Italicus domo Reginensis annorum XXXX Fabia Campana uxor marito merenti fecit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001042.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Rufinius Primus Italicus of Reginensium, aged 40, his wife Fabia Campana made this for her deserving husband. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001042.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Rufinius Primus Italicus, set up by his wife Fabia Campana, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09001043.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly a tombstone, bearing the names Bucco and Sibitta, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-09001044.text_edition: \n Lucius Calpurnius Saturninus annorum LV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001044.translation: Lucius Calpurnius Saturninus, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001044.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Lucius Calpurnius Saturninus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001046.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Cornelius Cornelianus annorum XXXVI hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001046.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cornelius Cornelianus, aged 36, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001046.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, recording the burial of Lucius Cornelius Cornelianus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001048.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Iunius Albanus annorum XXX mater pia faciendum curavit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001048.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Iunius Albanus, aged 30, is buried here. His devoted mother saw to it that this was made. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001048.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, recording the burial of Caius Iunius Albanus, with his mother as the dedicator, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001049.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcia Tycae sic nnorum LV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001049.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcia, daughter of Tycaeus, aged 55, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001049.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the Manes, recording the burial of Marcia, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001050.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Terentius Luci Terenti \n
EDCS-09001050.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Terentius, son of Lucius Terentius.
EDCS-09001050.summary: A funerary epitaph fragment dedicated to the Manes, recording the name of Lucius Terentius and his father, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001051.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription recording two individuals, one aged 80, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001059.text_edition: \n Cassia Suriaci filia Exorata annorum XXXII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Quintus Saenius Crescens uxori de suo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-09001059.translation: Cassia Suriaci, named Exorata, aged 32, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Quintus Saenius Crescens had this made for his wife at his own expense.
EDCS-09001059.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Cassia Exorata, erected by her husband Quintus Saenius Crescens, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001060.text_edition: \n Marcus Ver M hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001060.translation: Marcus Ver M is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001060.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Marcus Ver M, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001062.text_edition: \n Marcus Aellius sic Gallus annorum XIIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001062.translation: Marcus Aellius Gallus, aged 14, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001062.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Marcus Aellius Gallus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001063.text_edition: \n Marcus Domitius Boutius hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001063.translation: Marcus Domitius Boutius is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001063.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Marcus Domitius Boutius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001066.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Dasumius Eros annorum XXX hic situs est te rogo praeteriens dicas sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001066.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Dasumius Eros, aged 30, is buried here. I ask you, as you pass by, say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.'
EDCS-09001066.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Dasumius Eros, with a common epitaph formula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001067.text_edition: \n Vibia Aurelia Rufinio Silvano marito optimo annorum XXXX uxor pientissima fecit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09001067.translation: Vibia Aurelia dedicated this to her most excellent husband, Rufinius Silvanus, aged 40. His most dutiful wife made this. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09001067.summary: A tombstone erected by a devoted wife for her husband, Rufinius Silvanus, indicating his age and burial location, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09001422.text_edition: \n Divo Augusto Quintus Fabius Quinti filius Varus pontifex Vvir sic cryptam hypaetrum de sua pecunia d(edit) dedicavit\n
EDCS-09001422.translation: Quintus Fabius Varus, son of Quintus, pontifex and duovir, gave and dedicated this crypt and open-air structure from his own funds to the deified Augustus.
EDCS-09001422.summary: A dedication of a public building (crypt and open-air structure) to the deified Augustus by a local magistrate, Quintus Fabius Varus, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-09300268.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi Traiani Parthici filius divi Nervae nepos Traianus Hadrianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XX consul III pater patriae restituit\n
EDCS-09300268.translation: The Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the deified Nerva, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 20th time, consul for the 3rd time, father of his country, restored (this).
EDCS-09300268.summary: An inscription recording the restoration of a building or structure by Emperor Hadrian, listing his imperial titles and powers, dating to approximately 117-118 AD.

EDCS-09300269.text_edition: \n Imperatori Flavio Valerio Maximino nobilissimo Caesari (milia passuum) VI\n
EDCS-09300269.translation: To the Emperor, Caesar, Flavius Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesar, (for) 6 miles.
EDCS-09300269.summary: An inscription indicating a distance marker (6 miles) dedicated to Emperor Maximinus, likely related to a road or public work, from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-09300271.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Crispino sic nobilissimo Caesari\n
EDCS-09300271.translation: To our lord Crispinus, thus, to the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-09300271.summary: A dedication to Crispinus, identified as a 'most noble Caesar', suggesting a junior emperor or a significant figure, likely from the late Roman Empire.

EDCS-09401088.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Valerio Aurelio Diocletiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate X consuli VIII patri patriae proconsuli et Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Valerio Maximiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate VIII consuli VI patri patriae proconsuli et Flavio Valerio Constantio et Galerio Valerio Maximiano nobilissimi sic Caesares sic constituerunt\n
EDCS-09401088.translation: The Emperors Caesar Marcus Valerius Aurelius Diocletianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 10th time, consul for the 8th time, father of his country, proconsul, and Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Valerius Maximianus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 8th time, consul for the 6th time, father of his country, proconsul, and Flavius Valerius Constantius and Galerius Valerius Maximianus, most noble Caesars, thus established this.
EDCS-09401088.summary: A joint decree or establishment by Emperors Diocletian and Maximian, along with their Caesars Constantius and Galerius Maximian, detailing their extensive imperial titles, dating to around 296-300 AD.

EDCS-09700605.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Annia Leucippe Tritiae servae annorum XII faciendum curavit S T B hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09700605.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Annia Leucippe had this made for Tritia, a slave girl aged 12. [Restored: May she rest in peace.] She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09700605.summary: A funerary inscription for a young slave girl, Annia Leucippe, who died at age 12, with the work commissioned by another individual and a common epitaph formula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09701493.text_edition: \n Licinius Primus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-09701493.translation: Licinius Primus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-09701493.summary: A tombstone marking the burial of Licinius Primus, with a standard epitaph, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001235.text_edition: \n Caius Annius Felix annorum Annia Grata annorum L Annia Polla annorum XXX te rogo praeteriens dicas sit tibi terra levis hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-10001235.translation: Gaius Annius Felix, aged [ ], Annia Grata, aged 50, Annia Polla, aged 30. I ask you, as you pass by, say 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.' They are buried here.
EDCS-10001235.summary: A funerary inscription listing three individuals (Gaius Annius Felix, Annia Grata, and Annia Polla) with their ages (one incomplete) and indicating they are buried together, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10001238.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum L Theophile vixit annos LXVIII Aetolis patronae optimae fecit hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10001238.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Theophilus lived for 68 years. Aetolis made this for her excellent patroness. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001238.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Theophilus, dedicated by Aetolis, his patroness, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001240.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pinnia Helpis pia in suis \n
EDCS-10001240.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Pinnia Helpis, dutiful, among her own people.
EDCS-10001240.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pinnia Helpis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001256.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Romula annorum XXXX hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Fabius us contubernali de s(uo) p(osuit)\n
EDCS-10001256.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Romula, 40 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Fabius placed this from his own resources for his comrade.
EDCS-10001256.summary: A funerary epitaph for Romula, placed by Fabius, her comrade, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001279.translation: Vit() Romules/ia, 60 years old, rests here.
EDCS-10001279.summary: A funerary epitaph for an individual named Romules/ia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001284.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly a tomb marker or a dedication, for Diona and her children, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001296.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Fulvius Musicus nnum XXXXV hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10001296.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Fulvius Musicus, 45 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001296.summary: A funerary epitaph for Quintus Fulvius Musicus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001298.text_edition: \n Licinia Cai filia Mancina nnorum XV hic sita est sit tibi terra levis Caius Licinius Galeria Lupus hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10001298.translation: Licinia Mancina, daughter of Caius, 15 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Gaius Licinius Lupus lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001298.summary: A funerary epitaph commemorating Licinia Mancina and Gaius Licinius Lupus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001328.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelius Epaenetus nnum LXX optumus pater optumus amicus optumus rector pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10001328.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Epaenetus, 70 years old, an excellent father, an excellent friend, an excellent leader, dutiful, among his own people, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10001328.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelius Epaenetus, praising his character and familial relationships, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001347.translation: To our Lord Flavius Claudius Constantinus Iunior, noblest Caesar, to Constantinus, most noble, dutiful, fortunate Augustus.
EDCS-10001347.summary: A dedication to Emperor Constantine Iunior and Emperor Constantinus, likely dating to the 4th century AD.

EDCS-10001352.text_edition: \n Marcus Cassius Marci filius Galeria Sempronianus Olisiponensis diffusor olearius a solo fecit et dedicavit\n
EDCS-10001352.translation: Marcus Cassius, son of Marcus, Galeria Sempronianus, from Olisipo, oil merchant, made and dedicated from the ground up.
EDCS-10001352.summary: A dedication by Marcus Cassius Sempronianus, an oil merchant from Olisipo (Lisbon), likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10001353.text_edition: \n Cassia Zoe annorum XXXVII hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10001353.translation: Cassia Zoe, aged 37, is laid here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-10001353.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cassia Zoe, recording her age and a standard wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-10001354.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Iunius Vernaclus nnorum XXII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10001354.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcus Iunius Vernaclus, aged 22, is laid here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-10001354.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Iunius Vernaclus, recording his age and a standard wish for the afterlife.

EDCS-10001356.text_edition: \n Proserpinae sacrum ex voto N Tuccius Narcissus\n
EDCS-10001356.translation: Sacred to Proserpina, in fulfillment of a vow by N. Tuccius Narcissus.
EDCS-10001356.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Proserpina, made in fulfillment of a vow by N. Tuccius Narcissus.

EDCS-10001357.text_edition: \n Sacrum sanctae Deae Diadumenus servus Caesaris nostri animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-10001357.translation: Sacred to the holy Goddess. Diadumenus, servant of our Emperor, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10001357.summary: This is a votive inscription to a 'holy Goddess', dedicated by Diadumenus, a servant of the Emperor.

EDCS-10001365.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Terentius Firmus nnorum LXI pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10001365.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Terentius Firmus, aged 61, pious in his family, is laid here; may the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-10001365.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Terentius Firmus, recording his age and emphasizing his piety.

EDCS-10100002.text_edition: \n Apollini Augusto sacrum Marcus Sentius Marci filius Sergia Maurianus Italicensis aedilis IIvir augur perpetuus coloniae Aeliae Augustae Italicae ex argenti pondo C dono dedit\n
EDCS-10100002.translation: Dedicated to Apollo Augustus. Marcus Sentius Maurianus, son of Marcus, of the Sergia tribe, from Italica, aedile, twice aedile, perpetual augur of the colony Aelia Augusta Italica, gave this gift of 100 pounds of silver.
EDCS-10100002.summary: This is a dedication of silver to Apollo Augustus by Marcus Sentius Maurianus, an official of the colony of Italica, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10100003.text_edition: \n Marcus Cassius Sergia Caecilianus flamen perpetuus divi Traiani flaminalis provinciae Baeticae statuas quae sunt in ordinem positae numero IIII ex argenti libris centenis ob honorem IIviratus Genio coloniae splendidissimae Italicensis posuit\n
EDCS-10100003.translation: Marcus Cassius Caecilianus, perpetual priest of the deified Trajan and priest of the province Baetica, set up four statues, arranged in a row, each weighing 100 pounds of silver, in honor of his office as twice aedile and for the Genius of the most splendid colony of Italica.
EDCS-10100003.summary: This inscription records Marcus Cassius Caecilianus commissioning four silver statues for the colony of Italica in honor of his priesthoods and office, likely dating to the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10100006.text_edition: \n Caelesti Piae Augustae Caius Servilius Africanus cum liberis animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-10100006.translation: To the Heavenly and Pious Empress. Gaius Servilius Africanus, with his children, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100006.summary: This is a votive inscription to an Empress, dedicated by Gaius Servilius Africanus and his children.

EDCS-10100007.translation: To Nemesis the Present. Aurelius Polyticus.
EDCS-10100007.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Nemesis by Aurelius Polyticus.

EDCS-10100009.text_edition: \n Dominae Regiae Publius B Fortunatus sacerdos coloniae Aeliae Augustae Italicensium\n
EDCS-10100009.translation: To the Royal Lady. Publius B. Fortunatus, priest of the colony of Aelia Augusta of the people of Italica.
EDCS-10100009.summary: This is a dedication to a 'Royal Lady' by Publius B. Fortunatus, a priest of the colony of Italica.

EDCS-10100015.text_edition: \n Victoriae Augustae Vibia Modesta Cai Vibi Libonis filia oriunda Mauretania iterato honore bis flaminica sacerdos statuam argenteam ex argenti pondo CXXXII |(unciarum duarum semunciae) cum inauribus tribacis margaritis numero X et gemmis numero XXXX et berullis numero VIII et corona aurea cum gemmis numero XXV et gemmareis sic C accepto loco ab splendidissimo ordine in templo suo coronam auream flaminalem capitulum aureum dominae Isidis alterum Cereris cum maanilibus sic argenteis item Iunonis Reginae dono dedit\n
EDCS-10100015.translation: To Imperial Victory, Vibia Modesta, daughter of Caius Vibius Libo, a native of Mauretania, twice flaminica priestess, having received repeated honor, dedicated a silver statue weighing 132 pounds, two ounces, and a half-ounce, along with earrings with trefoils, ten pearls, forty gems, eight beryls, and a gold crown with twenty-five gems and twenty-five gold settings. In appreciation for the esteemed order, she also dedicated in her temple a gold flaminica crown, a gold head-dress for the Lady Isis, another for Ceres with silver fittings, and likewise one for Juno Regina.
EDCS-10100015.summary: This inscription records Vibia Modesta's dedication of a silver statue and various adornments to the goddess Victoria Augusta, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10100021.summary: This inscription records Emperor Hadrian as the builder of a structure, likely dating to his reign (117-138 CE).

EDCS-10100026.text_edition: \n Lucius Herius Luci filius IIvir iterum IIvirali potestate decreto decurionum tertium pontifex creatus Augusto primus municipio pollicitus ex patrimonio suo arcus porticus de sua pecunia dedit idemque dedicavit\n
EDCS-10100026.translation: Lucius Herius, son of Lucius, twice chief magistrate, again in the power of chief magistrate, appointed pontifex for the third time by decree of the decurions, promised Augustus and was the first in the municipality, and gave from his own patrimony arches and porticoes, which he himself dedicated.
EDCS-10100026.summary: This inscription records Lucius Herius's civic benefactions of arches and porticoes funded by his personal wealth, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-10100027.text_edition: \n Lucius Blattius Luci filius Traianus Pollio Caius Cai filius Pollio IIviri designati iterum pontifices primi creati Augusto orchestram proscaenium itinera aras et signa de sua pecunia faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-10100027.translation: Lucius Blattius, son of Lucius, Traianus Pollio, and Caius, son of Caius, Pollio, designated chief magistrates, appointed pontifices for the second time, took care to have built from their own funds the orchestra, the stage, passages, altars, and statues.
EDCS-10100027.summary: This inscription records the joint civic benefactions of two designated chief magistrates, Lucius Blattius Traianus Pollio and Caius Pollio, who funded the construction of theatrical and religious structures, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10100071.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Licinia Maurilla, aged 31, dutiful to her family, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100071.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licinia Maurilla, recording her age and a wish for a light burial, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100142.text_edition: \n Lucius Apronius Luci libertus Praestus annorum XIIX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10100142.translation: Lucius Apronius Praestus, freedman of Lucius, aged 18, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100142.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Apronius Praestus, recording his age and a wish for a light burial, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100154.translation: I am placed, with Bishop Domitius safe. Amen.
EDCS-10100154.summary: This inscription, possibly part of a mosaic or pavement, records the safety of Bishop Domitius and includes a Christian invocation, likely from the later Roman or early Byzantine period.

EDCS-10100157.text_edition: \n Protoginia sic annorum XX mensium X hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10100157.translation: Protoginia, aged 20 years and 10 months, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100157.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Protoginia, recording her age and a wish for a light burial, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100160.text_edition: \n Marcus Fabius Myro hic situs est\n
EDCS-10100160.translation: Marcus Fabius Myro is buried here.
EDCS-10100160.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Marcus Fabius Myro, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100170.text_edition: \n Genio municipi Artigiensis Lucius Fabius Quinti filius Galeria C Artigiensis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100170.translation: To the genius of the municipality of Artigiensis, Lucius Fabius, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, of Artigiensis, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10100170.summary: This inscription records Lucius Fabius's fulfillment of a vow to the genius of the municipality of Artigiensis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100224.text_edition: \n Gnaeo Servilio Gnaei filio Galeria Nigro IIviro interregi\n
EDCS-10100224.translation: To Gnaeus Servilius Niger, son of Gnaeus, duovir.
EDCS-10100224.summary: This is a dedication to Gnaeus Servilius Niger, who held the office of duovir, likely related to a public building or monument.

EDCS-10100245.text_edition: \n Marco Messio Marci filio Galeria Rusticiano Aemilio Lepido Iulio Celso Balbino Arrio Proculo tribuno plebis designato quaestori Imperatoris Caesaris Titi Aelii Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae tribuno militum legionis XV Apollinaris et legionis III Gallicae praefecto urbis feriarum Latinarum consulatu Aemili Papi patris Burbulei Ligariani soceri Xviro stlitibus iudicandis res publica Siarensium Aemilius Papus pater honore usus sua pecunia posuit\n
EDCS-10100245.translation: To Marcus Messius Rusticiano, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile; to Aemilius Lepidus Iulius Celsus Balbus Arrius Proculus, tribune of the plebs elect, quaestor of the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the country, military tribune of the XV Apollinaris and III Gallica legions, prefect of the city during the Latin Festival, and father-in-law of Burbuleius Ligarianus; Aemilius Papus, his father, erected this at his own expense.
EDCS-10100245.summary: This is a funerary or honorific inscription set up by Aemilius Papus for his son, listing the extensive public and military career of the deceased Marcus Messius Rusticiano, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100250.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Postumia Silana hic sita est annorum XXX pia parentibus amatrix municipi Postumia Sura mater dedit\n
EDCS-10100250.translation: To the Divine Shades. Postumia Silana, aged 30, dutiful to her parents, devoted to her town, lies here. Postumia Sura, her mother, dedicated this.
EDCS-10100250.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Postumia Silana, set up by her mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100269.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Fabia Virginia, aged 38, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-10100269.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fabia Virginia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10102150.text_edition: \n Imperator Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIIII imperator XIIII consul VII pater patriae salutem dicit IIIIviris et decurionibus Muniguensibus cum ideo appellaveritis ne pecuniam quam debebatis Servilio Pollioni ex sententia Semproni Fusci solveretis poenam iniustae appellationis exsigi sic a vobis oportebat sed ego malui cum indulgentia mea quam cum temeritati sic vestra loqui et sestertia quinquaginta millia sic nummorum tenuitati publicae quam praetexitis remisi scripsi autem Gallicano amico meo proconsuli pecuniam quae adiudicata est Pollioni numerassetis ex die sententiae dictae usurarum vos conputationem sic liberaret reditus vectigaliorum vestrorum quae conducta habuisse Pollionem indicatis in rationem venire aequom sic est ne quid hoc nomine rei publicae apsit sic vale datum VII Idus Septembres\n
EDCS-10102150.translation: Emperor Titus Caesar Vespasianus Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 9th time, imperator for the 14th time, consul for the 7th time, father of the country, sends greetings to the duoviri and decurions of Muniguensia. Because you appealed to me so that you would not pay the money you owed to Servilius Pollio, in accordance with the judgment of Sempronius Fuscus, the penalty for unjust appeal should have been exacted from you. But I preferred to speak with my indulgence rather than with your rashness. And I have remitted fifty thousand sesterces of the public treasury, which you claim as debt, out of my generosity. I have written to my friend Gallicanus, proconsul, that you should pay the money adjudicated to Pollio from the day the sentence was given, and that you should be freed from the calculation of interest. It is fair that the revenues of your public property, which you indicate Pollio has farmed, should be brought into the account, so that nothing in this regard is lacking to the public good. Farewell. Issued on the 7th day before the Ides of September.
EDCS-10102150.summary: This is an imperial rescript issued by Emperor Vespasian to the town of Muniguensia regarding a debt owed to Servilius Pollio, remitting a penalty and settling interest payments, likely dating to the Flavian period (AD 69-96).

EDCS-10102151.text_edition: \n Sextus Curvius Silvinus quaestor pro praetore hospitium fecit cum senatu populoque Muniguensi Hispaniae ulterioris eosque liberos posterosque eorum in fidem clientelamque suam liberorum posterorumque suorum recepit egerunt Lucius Lucceius Luci filius magistratus legatus Lucius Octavius Marci filius Silvanus\n
EDCS-10102151.translation: Sextus Curvius Silvinus, quaestor pro praetore, made a treaty of hospitality with the senate and people of Muniguensia in Further Spain, and received them and their children and descendants into his protection and patronage, along with his own children and descendants. The treaty was managed by Lucius Lucceius, son of Lucius, as magistrate's legate, and Lucius Octavius Silvanus, son of Marcus.
EDCS-10102151.summary: This inscription records a formal hospitality pact (hospitium) between Sextus Curvius Silvinus, a Roman official, and the town of Muniguensia in Hispania Ulterior, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10200155.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Maternus, a deserving public slave, aged 70, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you. Sacred to the Divine Shades. Paternus, deserving dispensor, aged 97, lies here. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-10200155.summary: This is a double funerary epitaph for two deserving slaves, Maternus and Paternus, one a public slave and the other a 'dispensator' (financial administrator), dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701152.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Marci filius Galeria Abbicus miles legionis X\n
EDCS-11701152.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Abbicus, son of Marcus, of the Galeria tribe, soldier of the X legion.
EDCS-11701152.summary: This is an inscription likely honoring Marcus Aurelius Abbicus, a soldier of the Tenth Legion, possibly part of a dedication or a milestone.

EDCS-11701189.text_edition: \n Lucius Rutilius Titi filius Decimus Marcus Valerius Titi filius aedilis municipii Flavi \n
EDCS-11701189.translation: To Lucius Rutilius, son of Titus, and Decimus Valerius, son of Titus, aediles of the municipium Flavium.
EDCS-11701189.summary: This inscription records the names of two aediles, Lucius Rutilius and Decimus Valerius, of the municipium Flavium, likely related to a public work or dedication.

EDCS-11701192.text_edition: \n Iovi Optumo Maxumo sacrum Lucius Vibius Vegetus animo libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-11701192.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Lucius Vibius Vegetus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-11701192.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, dedicated by Lucius Vibius Vegetus who willingly fulfilled a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-11701225.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Junius Hebenus, son of Lucius, an Augustalis official of 65 years, had this made for himself and his wife, Valeria Restituta. Here they lie. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Valeria Restituta, 40 years old, lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Traveler, I beg you, as you pass, say: may the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701225.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the burial of Lucius Junius Hebenus and his wife Valeria Restituta, dated by the Augustalis title.

EDCS-11701231.text_edition: \n vixit nnos XLI Titinius Chrestus et Titinia Urnia patri pientissimo posuerunt hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11701231.translation: Having lived 41 years, Titinius Chrestus and Titinia Urnia set this up for their most devoted father. He lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701231.summary: A funerary inscription recording the dedication of a monument by Titinius Chrestus and Titinia Urnia to their father.

EDCS-11701253.translation: To Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva.
EDCS-11701253.summary: A dedication to Emperor Hadrian, listing his imperial titulature.

EDCS-11701258.translation: Segumarus, son of Talabarus, lies here.
EDCS-11701258.summary: A simple funerary inscription recording the burial of Segumarus.

EDCS-11701298.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Marci filia Fundana nnorum XXVIII Augustianus maritus uxori pientissimae faciendum curavit hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-11701298.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Julia Fundana, daughter of Marcus, 28 years old, had this made for her most devoted wife, Augustiana. She lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-11701298.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the burial of Julia Fundana and her wife Augustiana, with the latter's husband commissioning the monument.

EDCS-11800621.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Siciniae Quinti filiae Tuscillae\n
EDCS-11800621.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed for Sicinia Tuscilla, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-11800621.summary: A funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Sicinia Tuscilla.

EDCS-11800877.text_edition: \n Ex figlinis Tur Seiae Isauricae opus doliare ab Lucio Fadio Pass--- Serviano III et Varo consulibus\n
EDCS-11800877.translation: From the potteries of Turseia Isaurica, a tile-making work by Lucius Fadius Pass[---] Servianus, during the consulships of III and Varus.
EDCS-11800877.summary: An inscription from a tile manufactory, mentioning the owner and the consuls for dating purposes.

EDCS-11800879.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Stertinia Superata annorum XIIII mensium X dierum XII horum II hic sita est sit tibi terra levis pia in suis posuit aram Marius Veranus maritus suae\n
EDCS-11800879.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Stertinia Superata, aged 14 years, 10 months, and 12 days (of which 2 were spent in the womb), lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Her devoted husband, Marius Veranus, placed this altar for his beloved wife.
EDCS-11800879.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Stertinia Superata and mentioning her husband Marius Veranus who erected the altar.

EDCS-11800881.text_edition: \n Lucius Manilius L T libertus Niger nnorum XXVIII pius in suis hic situs est sit tibi terra levis Clodia Luci filia Augustina Olaurensis pientissima adsidua faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-11800881.translation: Lucius Manilius Niger, freedman of Lucius, 28 years old, pious in his own [tomb], lies here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Clodia Augustina, daughter of Lucius, from Olauris, most devoted and constant, had this made.
EDCS-11800881.summary: A tombstone inscription recording the burial of Lucius Manilius Niger, commissioned by Clodia Augustina.

EDCS-11800884.summary: A name inscribed, likely part of a funerary monument or dedication.

EDCS-13302669.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodia Fausta, identifying her by her father's name and filial tribe.

EDCS-13400026.text_edition: \n Saluti Augusti Lucius Petronius Luci libertus \n
EDCS-13400026.translation: To Augustus, by Lucius Petronius, freedman of Lucius.
EDCS-13400026.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Augustus, set up by his freedman Lucius Petronius.

EDCS-13700423.text_edition: \n Lucanus Fedeles sic dominae curatrici animae\n
EDCS-13700423.translation: Lucanus Fedeles to his lady guardian of his soul.
EDCS-13700423.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a female guardian, possibly a patron or freedwoman, by Lucanus Fedeles.

EDCS-16000284.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto tribunicia potestate proconsuli Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi pontificis maximi fortissimi felicissimi patris patriae filio divi Marci Antonini Germanici Sarmatici nepoti divi Antonini Pii pronepoti divi Hadriani abnepoti divi Traiani Parthici et divi Nervae adnepoti res publica Murgitana\n
EDCS-16000284.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, holding tribunician power, proconsul, son of the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus, chief priest, most powerful, most fortunate, father of the fatherland, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus Germanicus Sarmaticus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva, for the Republic of Murgitana.
EDCS-16000284.summary: This is a formal dedication by the Republic of Murgitana to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (likely Caracalla), listing his full imperial titles and ancestry.

EDCS-16300027.summary: This is a dedication to the Caenicorum, a group or deity, likely by a local community.

EDCS-16300381.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius divi Iuli nepos Augustus pontifex maximus XXI consul V imperator VIII tribunicia potestate XXXVII ab Iano Augusto et Baetem sic usque ad Oceanum LXXVIII\n
EDCS-16300381.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, chief priest, consul for the 21st time, emperor for the 8th time, holding tribunician power for the 37th time, from Janus Augustus and Baetis up to the Ocean, 78 [paces/miles].
EDCS-16300381.summary: This inscription records the extent of an accomplishment or territory, possibly related to a building project or military campaign, attributed to Emperor Tiberius and referencing geographical markers.

EDCS-16400022.translation: To Mars the lord, by the Republic of Murgensium.
EDCS-16400022.summary: This is a dedication to the god Mars by the community of Murgensium.

EDCS-16400434.translation: To the divine power of Silvanus, in fulfillment of a vow by Modestinus.
EDCS-16400434.summary: This is a votive offering to the god Silvanus, made by Modestinus.

EDCS-16400449.translation: ... lived for ... years and Fortuna[...]. The freedman lived for 16 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-16400449.summary: This fragmented epitaph records the lifespan of a freedman and includes a wish for his peaceful rest.

EDCS-16900175.text_edition: \n Maximus Nigriano et hoc fuit providentia actoris ut puellam quae iam feto sic tollerat sic mitteres illam ac tale labore ut mancipius sic dominicus sic periret qui tam magno labori factus sic fuerat sic et hoc Maxima fecit Trofimiani sic fota et castiga illum quasi ex omni closus sic est \n
EDCS-16900175.translation: Maximus did this also for Nigrianus, and it was by the actor's foresight that he let go of the girl who was already carrying the fetus, and that he perished in such labor as if a slave and domestic, who had been made for such great labor. And this Maxima also did it, fostered and corrected him as if he were completely enclosed.
EDCS-16900175.summary: This inscription is a complex and difficult to interpret account of events involving Maximus, Nigrianus, a girl with a fetus, and Maxima, possibly relating to a birth, death, or manumission.

EDCS-16900219.text_edition: \n Publicia coloniae Iuliae Augustae Emeritae libertae Graecula hic sita est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-16900219.translation: Publicia Graecula, freedwoman of the colony of Iulia Augusta Emerita, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-16900219.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Publicia Graecula, a freedwoman in Emerita, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900220.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Of Bouti[...]
EDCS-16900220.summary: This fragmentary inscription is a dedication to the underworld deities, beginning the name of the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900225.translation: Areni, son of Lubaecus, is laid here. Duanna(?), daughter of Arreinus, is laid here.
EDCS-16900225.summary: This epitaph records the burial of two individuals, Areni and Duanna(?), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900226.translation: [...] Sympheronti, aged 50, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you. Iulia Tusca, daughter of Gaius, erected this for her most devoted father.
EDCS-16900226.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Sympheronti, aged 50, with the monument erected by his daughter Iulia Tusca, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-16900386.text_edition: \n Lucio Cornelio Luci- Corneli Iunia adsidua \n
EDCS-16900386.translation: To Lucius Cornelius, son of Lucius Cornelius. Iunia adsidua [...]
EDCS-16900386.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication or memorial to Lucius Cornelius, son of Lucius Cornelius, with Iunia adsidua involved, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-18600001.text_edition: \n Marcus Helvius Sabinus Metellinensis annorum LX hic situs est sit tibi terra levis sodales tabulae salutaris faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-18600001.translation: Marcus Helvius Sabinus of Metellinum, aged 60, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you. The members of the 'tabula salutaris' saw to its erection.
EDCS-18600001.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Marcus Helvius Sabinus, aged 60, and was erected by his colleagues from the 'tabula salutaris', likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-19100787.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Achaicus ISI IA CETA VII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-19100787.translation: Marcus Aurelius Achaicus, aged 7 [...], is laid here; may the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-19100787.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Marcus Aurelius Achaicus, aged 7, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20301597.translation: By the authority of Quintus Iunius Rusticus, prefect of the city.
EDCS-20301597.summary: This inscription records an official decree or action taken by Quintus Iunius Rusticus, prefect of the city, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20301610.text_edition: \n Marcus Petrucidius Marci filius legatus pro praetore Marcus Licinius\n
EDCS-20301610.translation: Marcus Petrucidius, son of Marcus, legate pro praetore. Marcus Licinius [...]
EDCS-20301610.summary: This inscription mentions Marcus Petrucidius, a legate pro praetore, and Marcus Licinius, likely recording a legal or administrative matter from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20301621.summary: This inscription refers to an action or decree related to Gnaeus Magnus, who is identified as an emperor, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-20400265.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, invested with tribunician power.
EDCS-20400265.summary: This inscription records titles of Emperor Decius, likely part of a larger monument, dating to his reign in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-20400355.summary: This inscription records that Emperor Hadrian built something, likely a public work, dating to his reign (117-138 CE).

EDCS-20400356.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Caius Pontius Alexi, likely on a tomb, the date is uncertain.

EDCS-20400358.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Ugaiddillus vixit annos XXIII frater Musici hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20400358.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Ugaiddillus lived for 23 years. His brother Musicius is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20400358.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Aurelius Ugaiddillus, who lived 23 years, and is dedicated by his brother Musicius, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20400359.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pontius Caecilius Donatus vixit nnos XXXI menses V dies VIIII fratres pientissimi fecerunt hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20400359.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Pontius Caecilius Donatus lived 31 years, 5 months, and 9 days. His most dutiful brothers made this. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20400359.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Pontius Caecilius Donatus, who lived 31 years, 5 months, and 9 days, and was dedicated by his brothers, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-20400366.translation: To our Lord Flavius Valerius Constantinus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, son of the deified Constantinus, born good for the republic.
EDCS-20400366.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Constantine the Great with various titles, likely part of a public monument and dating to his reign (306-337 CE).

EDCS-20401595.text_edition: \n Sacrum domus Augustae Marcus Clodius Galeria Proculus IIvir pontifex Augusti per honorem civitatem Romanam consecutus cum Annia uxore et Marco Clodio Rustico et Marco Clodio Marcello filiis beneficio Imperatoris Caesaris Vespasiani Augusti de sua pecunia dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-20401595.translation: Sacred to the imperial household. Marcus Clodius Galeria Proculus, duovir, priest of Augustus, having obtained Roman citizenship by virtue of his office, with his wife Annia and his sons Marcus Clodius Rusticus and Marcus Clodius Marcellus, dedicated and consecrated this at his own expense by the favor of the Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus.
EDCS-20401595.summary: This inscription records that Marcus Clodius Galeria Proculus, a local magistrate, received Roman citizenship under Emperor Vespasian and dedicated this monument at his own expense with his family, dating to Vespasian's reign (69-79 CE).

EDCS-20401597.text_edition: \n Lucius Pontius Luci filius Papiria Aquila praetorianus cohortis IIII hic situs sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-20401597.translation: Lucius Pontius Aquila, son of Lucius, of the Papirian tribe, a praetorian of the 4th cohort, is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-20401597.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Lucius Pontius Aquila, a member of the Praetorian Guard, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-21300315.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Pius Felix Invictus Augustus, chief priest.
EDCS-21300315.summary: This inscription records titles of Emperor Trebonianus Gallus, likely part of a larger monument, dating to his reign (251-253 CE).

EDCS-21300316.translation: To our lord Emperor Flavius Magnentius Pius Felix Always Augustus.
EDCS-21300316.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Magnentius, likely part of a public monument, dating to his reign (350-353 CE).

EDCS-21700290.text_edition: \n Victor Caesaris servus de suo fecit\n
EDCS-21700290.translation: Victor, the emperor's slave, made this at his own expense.
EDCS-21700290.summary: This is a private dedication by Victor, a slave of Caesar, likely marking a building or structure he funded, possibly during the imperial period.

EDCS-21700291.text_edition: \n Mamullia mulieris liberta Fausta Quintus Fabius Quinti filius Fortunalis nnorum VIII hic situs est sit tibi terra levis hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-21700291.translation: Fausta, freedwoman of Mamullia, is buried here; she was 8 years old. May the earth lie lightly upon you; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-21700291.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fausta, the freedwoman of Mamullia, dating to the Roman Imperial period, specifying her age and that the burial plot is not inheritable.

EDCS-21700295.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus, son of the deified Severus Pius, grandson of the deified Marcus Antoninus, great-grandson of the deified Antoninus Pius, great-great-grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-great-great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus and the deified Nerva...
EDCS-21700295.summary: This is a dedication inscription from the reign of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius Augustus (reigned 161-180 CE), listing his extensive imperial titulature, likely for a public building or monument.

EDCS-21700296.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified one, consul for the 13th time, with tribunician power for the 21st time, pontifex maximus, from the Betis river and Janus Augustus to the Ocean...
EDCS-21700296.summary: This is a dedication inscription from the reign of Augustus (27 BCE - 14 CE), detailing his consulships and tribunician power, likely commemorating public works or boundaries.

EDCS-21700297.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified one, consul for the 13th time, with tribunician power for the 21st time, pontifex maximus, from the Betis river and Janus Augustus to the Ocean...
EDCS-21700297.summary: This is a dedication inscription from the reign of Augustus (27 BCE - 14 CE), detailing his consulships and tribunician power, likely commemorating public works or boundaries.

EDCS-21700298.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified one, consul for the 13th time, with tribunician power for the 21st time, pontifex maximus, from the Betis river and Janus Augustus to the Ocean...
EDCS-21700298.summary: This is a dedication inscription from the reign of Augustus (27 BCE - 14 CE), detailing his consulships and tribunician power, likely commemorating public works or boundaries.

EDCS-21700300.translation: [Emperor Nerva] Trajan Caesar Augustus Germanicus, son of the deified Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, consul for the 2nd time, father of the fatherland, repaired and restored roads corrupted by age.
EDCS-21700300.summary: This inscription records the repair and restoration of roads by Emperor Trajan (reigned 98-117 CE), likely placed on a roadside monument or bridge.

EDCS-21700303.translation: [Gaius Caesar] Germanicus, son of Germanicus Caesar, grandson of Tiberius Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, great-great-grandson of the deified Julius, Augustus, father of the fatherland, consul for the 2nd time, imperator, with tribunician power for the 2nd time, pontifex maximus, from the Betis river and Janus Augustus to the Ocean... 73.
EDCS-21700303.summary: This inscription details the imperial titulature of Caligula (reigned 37-41 CE), likely marking the boundary of a territory or a significant construction project.

EDCS-21700304.translation: Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the deified one, consul for the 13th time, with tribunician power for the 21st time, pontifex maximus, from the Betis river and Janus Augustus to the Ocean... 75. [Emperor Caesar] Domitian Augustus, son of the deified Vespasian...
EDCS-21700304.summary: This inscription commemorates imperial boundaries or works, referencing Augustus and concluding with a mention of Emperor Domitian, likely dating from the late 1st century CE.

EDCS-21700397.translation: [Emperor Caesar] Gaius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, Germanicus maximus, Dacicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, with tribunician power for the 3rd time, imperator for the 4th time, consul, father of the fatherland, proconsul, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Nobilissimus Caesar Germanicus maximus, Sarmaticus maximus, Dacicus maximus, son of Emperor Caesar Augustus, restored...
EDCS-21700397.summary: This joint dedication by Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, dating to their reigns (235-238 CE), records their restoration of something, likely public works or fortifications.

EDCS-21700400.text_edition: \n Domino Nostro Imperatori Flavio Valerio Constantio Nobilissimo Caesari\n
EDCS-21700400.translation: To our Lord Emperor Flavius Valerius Constantius, the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-21700400.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Constantius, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-21700468.translation: Galla Blasti [erected this] / Servilia Su[perati] [erected this]
EDCS-21700468.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record the names of two individuals, Galla Blasti and Servilia Superati, possibly as dedicants or patrons of a monument.

EDCS-22200157.translation: Emperor Caesar Lucius [Domitius] Aurelianus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, father of the country, consul, proconsul, restored [this].
EDCS-22200157.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something by Emperor Aurelian, likely in the late 3rd century CE, listing his titles and honours.

EDCS-22200160.translation: To Constantius, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, son of the deified Constantius Pius.
EDCS-22200160.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Constantius II, son of Constantine I, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-22200233.translation: Emperor Caesar Valerius Galerius Maximianus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus, chief priest, holding tribunician power, consul [did this].
EDCS-22200233.summary: This inscription names Emperor Galerius Maximianus and lists his imperial titles, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-22400457.translation: To Ceres Frugifera, sacred. The association of surveyors of Carmona and the century of Albores, Volces, Agstes, Ligyes; the association of surveyors of Segobium and the century of Badyes, Cinens, Bodnes, Armores; the association of surveyors of Hienippa and the century of Lides, Moeles, Hybres, Limes; the association of surveyors of Arva and the century of Isurgutes, Halos, Arvabores, Ores; the association of surveyors of Oducia and the century of Galles, Secus, Elpes, Hares; the association of surveyors of Muniguensa and the century of Daudes, Aves, Albodunes, Erques; the association of surveyors of Axatita and the century of Isines, Alebries, Lestes, Hybres; the association of surveyors of Obulcula and the century of Melges, Verges, Belges, Tornes; eight cities and the other peoples, public bodies, associations, centuries, have joyfully set this up at public expense for the increase of crops. Marcus Ulpius Strabo, son of Marcus, grandson of Lucius, great-grandson of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, IIIvir, augur, pontiff, dedicated it by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-22400457.summary: This monumental inscription records the joint dedication of a public work by multiple associations of surveyors, supported by eight cities and other communities, for agricultural prosperity, and was dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Strabo, a local magistrate and priest, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-22800162.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-22800162.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano optimo Augusto Germanico Dacico Parthhico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XIIX imperatori X consuli VI patri patriae res publica Saeponensium statuam triumphalem ex decreto decurionum dedit\n
